Timeline / Phases: Phase 1 Max Protection (wks 0-6): NWB, brace locked 0, patellar mobs, ROM 30-90 with valgus stress, quad sets/SLR in brace; Phase 2 Progressive (wks 6-12): progressive WB, bilateral CKC squats, hip strengthening, step-up, gym strengthening, stationary bike/walking/elliptical; Phase 3 Advanced (wks 12-20): full ROM, advance cardio, CKC bilateral to unilateral, pool running wk 16; Phase 4 Plyometric (wks 20-24): straight plane running progressing to lateral/rotational, plyometrics bilateral to unilateral, functional brace fitting, sports test
Notes: NWB x 6 wks (stricter than PCL); valgus stress during early ROM protects PLC repair from varus loading; no hyperextension throughout all phases; sports test required before competition clearance; one of the longer more conservative knee protocols

WB Status: WBAT with walker/crutches from DOS; advance to crutches when able; unprotected WB when no limp (wk 6+); D/C to cane if limp at wk 6
Key Restrictions: CPM 0-120 started DOS; ROM goals mandatory - notify MD immediately if not met (0-100 by DC, 0-110 wk 2, 0-120 wk 4); max expected ROM 0-135; extension emphasis equivalent to ACL rehab
Timeline / Phases: DOS (OOB to chair, WBAT walker/crutches, CPM 0-120, ankle pumps, quad sets, stair instructions, OT eval for home aids); POD 1-3 (OOB 2x/day, advance to crutches if able, CPM, ROM exercises, stair training); POD 6-30 (CKC, ROM: 0-110 wk 2, 0-120 wk 4); Wks 1-6 (crutch/cane per MD, stationary bike ASAP goal 15-20 min 3x/wk, aggressive CKC); Wks 6-24 (unprotected WB if no limp, gait training, stationary bike 20-30 min/day)
Notes: Comprehensive joint program; spinal anesthetic + femoral and sciatic nerve blocks; CPM started DOS; most patients DC POD 2; extension emphasized as aggressively as ACL; OT eval for home aids (reachers, toilet risers); stationary bike initiated ASAP

WB Status: PWB 40-60 lbs with crutches wk 1; WBAT / D/C crutches wk 2
Key Restrictions: Knee wrap on - do not remove unless instructed by MD; if effusion develops with activity reduce intensity and modify program; advance to plyometrics/running only if knee quiet with no effusion and patient pain-free
Timeline / Phases: Wk 1 (PWB 40-60 lbs, quad sets/SLR); Wk 2 (WBAT, D/C crutches, AROM flexion/extension if minimal effusion, patellar mobs/lateral retinacular stretches, edema control, foot/ankle mobility review); Wks 3-6 (progressive active extension, CKC strengthening and aerobic program if minimal effusion, hip abd/extension strengthening); Wks 6-12 (plyometrics/sports-specific agility/running if knee quiet, then dismiss to HEP)
Notes: Effusion is primary limiter of progression throughout; patellar mobilization and lateral retinacular stretching central to recovery; relatively quick recovery vs ligament reconstructions; dismiss to HEP at wk 12

WB Status: NWB x 6 wks; WB 25% per week in boot starting wk 7; unrestricted WB after wk 12 if radiographic union
Key Restrictions: Complete immobilization in boot/cast wks 1-6; boot with orthotic wks 7-12; ASO lace-up brace with medial strap and orthotic wks 12-24; orthotic in all shoes long-term; increased swelling/pain may indicate incomplete bone healing - reduce WB and therapy demands accordingly
Timeline / Phases: Pre-op training 1 wk before surgery; Phase I Immobilization (wks 0-6, NWB); PT begins after 6 wks immobilization; Phase II Early Rehab (wks 7-12, boot with orthotic, 25% WB/wk, AROM only first 2 wks then passive if needed, isometrics then progress CKC to OC); Phase III Brace/Return (wks 12-24, ASO brace, unrestricted WB if radiographic union, proprioception progression)
Notes: Reconstruction often includes osteotomies with bone healing requirements; PT delayed until 6 wks post-op; must achieve dorsiflexion and inversion ROM; strength goal: PF and inversion with single-leg stance; proprioception goal: single-leg balance eyes closed 20 seconds; orthotic required long-term in all footwear

Surgery Type: Arthroscopic and Open Rotator Cuff Repair
WB Status: N/A; sling and swathe full time wks 1-6; sling daytime / swathe for sleep wks 5-6; discontinued end of wk 6
Key Restrictions: No active shoulder motion until wk 5-6; no lifting with operative hand until wk 6-8; avoid full cuff stress 4 months; no ballistic activities or push/pull exercises long term
Timeline / Phases: Wks 1-2 (sling, hand/wrist/elbow ROM only); Wks 3-4 (PT begins, passive ROM); Wks 5-6 (AAROM, sling weaned); Wks 6-8 (AROM, eccentrics arm weight only); Wks 8-12 (progressive resistance up to 5 lbs); Wks 13-16 (HEP, up to 10 lbs); Wks 17-20 (up to 20 lbs); Wks 21-24 (up to 40 lbs); Wk 24+ (return to throwing/racket sports)
Notes: Throwing sports progression at 6-9 months; push-ups/pull-ups/bench press generally not advised long term for older patients due to recurrent tear risk

WB Status: N/A; sling and swathe full time day and night x 4 wks
Key Restrictions: Avoid ABD/ER combination x 8 wks to protect anterior inferior glenohumeral ligament complex; no bench press/flies/military press in ABD/ER position through wk 12-16
Timeline / Phases: Wks 0-4 (sling/swathe, isometrics); Wks 5-6 (AROM initiated); Wks 7-8 (progressive ROM, PREs begin); Wks 9-11 (up to 5 lbs); Wks 12-16 (no sports, up to 10 lbs); Month 4-6 (sport-specific progressions); Month 6+ (return to sports with surgeon approval)
Notes: Throwing sports: graduated toss program at 4 months; contact sports no earlier than 6 months; return to sport individualized per strength/ROM/repair security

WB Status: N/A; sling and swathe full time day and night x 4 wks
Key Restrictions: No IR past anterior axillary line (posterior repair precaution); avoid bench press/flies/military press/posterior capsular stretching x 6 months; no sports until 9 months post-op
Timeline / Phases: Wks 0-4 (sling/swathe, immobilization); Wks 5-6 (initiate AROM); Wks 6-8 (stretching/joint mob); Wks 9-12 (PRE); Wks 12-16 (advance PRE, no sports); Wks 17-24 (return to light activity)
Notes: Full ROM goal at 12 wks; no water skiing 1 year; unrestricted throwing and competitive swimming at 1 year; graded throwing program begins at 9 months

WB Status: N/A; lifting restricted to 5 lbs operative arm wks 0-4; 10 lbs max wks 5-6
Key Restrictions: Limit lifting to 5 lbs all planes wks 0-4; advance to 10 lbs wks 5-6; RTC eccentrics kept below 90 degrees early
Timeline / Phases: Wks 0-4 (AAROM to AROM, scapular control, RTC eccentrics); Wks 5-6 (PRE, functional patterns); Wks 7-12 (progressive strengthening, plyometrics, full recovery goal); Wks 12-24 (home maintenance)
Notes: Condensed protocol with no formal phase names or sling/immobilization instructions; goal is full recovery by 12 wks post-op

Surgery Type: Distal Bicep Tendon Repair — Single Incision Endo Button
WB Status: N/A; elbow hinge brace blocking extension ~20 degrees wks 1-6
Key Restrictions: Elbow hinge brace blocking extension at ~20 degrees through wk 6; weight limit 5 lbs at wk 6; no progressive strengthening until wks 9-10
Timeline / Phases: Wks 1-2 (edema control, AROM in brace); Wk 3 (scar management, brace continued); Wk 6 (brace D/C, passive extension begun); Wks 9-10 (progressive strengthening); Wk 12 (full use)
Notes: Listed under Shoulder and Arm section on Mountain Ortho website; single-incision endo button technique

WB Status: N/A; sling/immobilizer full time wks 1-4; discontinued at wk 4-5 unless MD instructs otherwise
Key Restrictions: No biceps resistance x 6 wks; avoid full stress to superior labrum and biceps x 4 months; no jogging/bouncing until 12 wks post-op
Timeline / Phases: Wks 1-4 (PROM, sling full time); Wks 5-6 (AAROM/AROM transition, sling D/C); Wk 7 (gentle biceps resistance); Wks 8-12 (progressive resistance up to 5 lbs); Wks 12-16 (up to 10 lbs, jogging begins); Wks 17-24 (return to sport with surgeon clearance)
Notes: Throwing: short toss at 4 months, full throwing at 6 months; racket sports baseline at 4 months, serving/overheads at 6 months

WB Status: N/A; sling and swathe full time initially; sling daytime only wks 2-4; discontinued with MD approval wks 4-6
Key Restrictions: No active IR until wk 6; ER within surgeon-defined limits through wk 4; elevation capped at 120 degrees until wk 6
Timeline / Phases: 7 phases: POD 1-2 (hospital); POD 3-10; Wks 2-4; Wks 4-6; Wks 6-8; Wks 8-12; Wks 12-24 (home program); progresses from passive ROM to full strengthening/conditioning by 6 months
Notes: Codman pendulums initiated early through wk 4; PREs introduced wks 6-8; elevation goal 140-160 degrees by wks 8-12

WB Status: Initially brace locked with crutches; WBAT when pain-free; allograft/HS autograft PWB x6 weeks
Key Restrictions: No activities causing locking or giving way; no pivoting
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 0-2), Phase II (Wks 3-5), Phase III (Wks 6-8), Phase IV (8-12+ wks return to sport)

WB Status: FWB throughout
Key Restrictions: Avoid: descending stairs, prolonged sitting, running, jumping
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 0-2 acute), Phase II (Wks 2-4 sub-acute), Phase III (Wks 4-8 remodeling)

WB Status: PWB wks 0-1; WBAT with crutches; brace locked until quad control achieved; FWB ~wks 3-6
Key Restrictions: Flexion limited initially; no pivoting; brace locked for ambulation
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 0-2), Phase II (Wks 3-6), Phase III (Wks 7-12), Phase IV (Wks 13-16 transitional), Phase V (Months 3-5 early RTS), Phase VI (Months 6+ unrestricted)

WB Status: FC lesions: TTWB (20-30%) x6-8 wks; PTG lesions: PWB (50%) x2 wks then WBAT; FWB Phase II
Key Restrictions: No impact exercises; avoid pivoting; no stair-master; no open chain knee extension for PTG lesions
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 0-8 protection), Phase II (Wks 8-12 progressive loading), Phase III (Wks 12-16 remodeling), Phase IV (Wks 16+ maturation)
Notes: Return to sport 6-12 months depending on lesion size/location

WB Status: NWB x2 weeks all lesions; partial WB per lesion location/size; FWB by 6-10 weeks
Key Restrictions: Hinged brace locked in extension; CPM immediately post-op; no impact until MD cleared
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 0-6), Phase II (Wks 6-12), Phase III (Months 3-5), Phase IV (Months 5-6 transitional), Phase V (Months 6+ early RTS), Phase VI (Months 8-12 unrestricted)
Notes: Protocol distinguishes between condylar and patellofemoral lesions with different WB progressions

WB Status: FWB throughout
Key Restrictions: Avoid loading knee in flexion; minimize: descending stairs, prolonged sitting, running, jumping
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 0-2 acute), Phase II (Wks 2-4 sub-acute), Phase III (Wks 4-6 late/chronic)

WB Status: No osteotomy: 25% WB → 50% → 75% → WBAT by wks 5-6 in unlocked brace; with osteotomy: TDWB in locked brace
Key Restrictions: PROM only wks 0-2 (0-40°); no active knee extension 40-70°; no repetitive closed chain knee flexion 40-70°; brace locked at 0° for ambulation
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 0-6), Phase II (Wks 6-12), Phase III (Wks 12-24), Phase IV (24+ wks advanced strengthening), Phase V (9-12 months early RTS), Phase VI (12 months unrestricted)
Notes: 4 stages of cartilage maturation; size/location of defect guides progression

WB Status: NWB/TDWB brace locked x4 wks; TDWB progressing to PWB wks 4-6; WBAT per MD based on X-ray
Key Restrictions: No active knee extension; no resisted exercise until 6 wks; no running/jumping/plyometrics until 4-6 months
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 0-6), Phase II (Wks 7-12 protection), Phase III (Wks 13-16 advanced strengthening), Phase IV (16+ wks early RTS)
Notes: Return to sport 7-9 months

WB Status: TDWB (20-30%) in locked brace x4 wks; WBAT with crutches in unlocked brace wks 5-6; discharge brace at 6 wks if SLR without lag
Key Restrictions: No closed chain knee flexion; no open chain extension; brace locked at 0° for WB x2 wks; CPM 6-8 hrs/day
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 0-6), Phase II (Wks 6-12), Phase III (Wks 12-24), Phase IV (Months 6-9), Phase V (Months 9-12 early RTS), Phase VI (Months 12-18 unrestricted)
Notes: Protocol varies by graft location: anterior vs posterior femoral condyle

WB Status: Initially brace locked with crutches; WBAT when pain/effusion-free with proper gait; allograft/HS autograft PWB x6 weeks
Key Restrictions: No pivot on surgical side; brace until 6 wks per MD; allograft/HS autograft conservative WB x6 wks; more conservative if concomitant meniscus repair
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 0-2), Phase II (Wks 3-5), Phase III (Wks 6-8), Phase IV (Wks 9-12 transitional), Phase V (Months 3-5 early RTS), Phase VI (Months 6+ unrestricted)
Notes: Different timelines for allograft vs hamstring autograft graft types

WB Status: Brace locked PWB with crutches wks 0-6; D/C brace/crutches at 6 wks per MD once adequate quad control and normalized gait
Key Restrictions: Flexion <90° wks 0-6; avoid active knee flexion (hamstring strain risk on posteromedial joint); no pivoting
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 0-3), Phase II (Wks 3-6), Phase III (Wks 6-8), Phase IV (Wks 9-12 transitional), Phase V (Months 3-5 early RTS), Phase VI (Months 6+ unrestricted)
Notes: Does not apply to meniscus root repairs or transplants; more conservative for complex tears/all-inside repairs

WB Status: Sling most of time wks 0-2; sleep in sling; wean wks 7-12
Key Restrictions: No active shoulder ROM; no passive ROM >90 deg; no reaching behind/across body; no lifting wks 0-6; no lifting >1lb wks 7-12; no lifting >2-3lbs wks 13-18; no overhead weighted lifting
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 0-6), Phase II (Wks 7-12), Phase III (Wks 13-18), Phase IV (Wks 19+ advanced strengthening)

WB Status: Sling at all times (neutral rotation, 30-45 deg ABD) wks 0-5; sleep in sling 6 wks; D/C sling wks 6-8
Key Restrictions: No AROM wks 0-5; no carrying objects until 12 wks; avoid ABD/ER; no lifting >10lbs wks 6-11; driving per MD at wk 6; no throwing/overhead until 4 months; avoid wide grip bench press, military press, lat pulldowns behind head
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 0-3 immediate), Phase II (Wks 4-5 protection), Phase III (Wks 6-8 intermediate), Phase IV (Wks 9-11 transitional), Phase V (Wks 12-16 strengthening), Phase VI (Months 4-6 unrestricted RTS)

WB Status: Sling per surgeon; wean starting wk 3
Key Restrictions: No AROM elbow or shoulder wks 1-4; no shoulder ER >40 deg; no shoulder extension past neutral; no lifting; no loading biceps/elbow flexors/supinators wks 4-6; avoid long-lever arm resistance for elbow flexion/supination wks 6-8; no swimming until wks 8+
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 1-4 PROM), Phase II (Wks 4-6 AROM), Phase III (Wks 6-8 initial strengthening), Phase IV (Wks 8-12 advanced strengthening), Phase V (Wks 12-16 RTS)

WB Status: Sling at night only; D/C wk 6 or per MD
Key Restrictions: No PROM ER past neutral until wk 5; no shoulder AROM until wk 5; no lifting objects; no supporting body weight; avoid excessive ER ROM stretching; avoid load on anterior capsule/subscapularis; limit overstressing anterior capsule with overhead strengthening
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 0-6 immediate), Phase II (Wks 7-8 intermediate), Phase III (Wks 9-12 late), Phase IV (Wks 13-20 strengthening), Phase V (Wks 21-28 early RTS)
Notes: Procedure for >20% glenoid bone loss; primary functional limitation is reduced glenohumeral ER; ER should be progressed gradually

WB Status: Sling in neutral/IR; sleep in sling; bone-tendon wean at wk 4; tendon-tendon/muscle-tendon wean wks 5-6; WB through surgical side at wks 6-8
Key Restrictions: Very limited PROM initially (ER at 0 deg ADD, flex 45 deg, ABD 30 deg); no shoulder AROM until wks 6-8; no IR/pec major loading until wks 9-14; progressive PROM increases 5-10 deg per week
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 0-3), Phase II (Wks 4-6), Phase III (Wks 6-8 late), Phase IV (Wks 9-14 transitional), Phase V (Wks 14-20 advanced strengthening), Phase VI (Months 5-6 early RTS), Phase VII (Months 6+ unrestricted RTS)
Notes: Repair type (bone-tendon vs tendon-tendon vs muscle-tendon) dictates sling weaning timeline; 50% 1RM bench press at 6+ months with physician coordination

WB Status: Sling x3 weeks (off 4x/day); sleep in sling until 6 wks; D/C sling at 6 wks; no WB through arm until wks 6-8
Key Restrictions: No active motion involved shoulder; no lifting/carrying; no WB; no flex/ABD >90 deg wks 1-4; no lifting >coffee cup wks 5-8; no lifting >5lbs wks 9-12
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 1-4 immediate), Phase II (Wks 5-8 intermediate), Phase III (Wks 9-12 late), Phase IV (Wks 12-16 transitional), Phase V (Months 4-6 RTS)

WB Status: Sling in neutral rotation with abduction pillow (30-45 deg); sleep in sling; wean during day wks 4-6; D/C at wks 7-8
Key Restrictions: No AROM/AAROM wks 2-3; no PROM into IR wks 2-3; no reaching behind back (especially into IR) throughout; no lifting >coffee cup wks 4-8; no WB through hands; avoid shoulder hyperextension; no lifting >10lbs wks 9-11; no lifting >15lbs wks 12-16
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 2-3 immediate), Phase II (Wks 4-6 intermediate), Phase III (Wks 7-8), Phase IV (Wks 9-11 transitional), Phase V (Wks 12-16 advanced strengthening)
Notes: Deltoid-driven rehab (not rotator cuff); distinct from TSA protocol; outcomes depend on surgical approach and whether RTC repair was performed

WB Status: Sling day and night with abduction pillow (30-45 deg) wks 1-6; gradually removed wks 6-10 with surgeon clearance
Key Restrictions: No AROM or PROM shoulder wks 1-6; no WB; no reaching overhead or behind back; no pushing/pulling; no AROM until wks 10+; no IR until wks 6-10; no lifting >5lbs wks 18-22; no lifting >10lbs wks 22-26; no empty can position at any stage; no straight arm lateral raise ever
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 1-6 immobilization), Phase II (Wks 6-10 PROM), Phase III (Wks 10-18 AAROM/AROM + isometrics wks 14-18), Phase IV (Wks 18-22 initial strengthening), Phase V (Wks 22-26 advanced strengthening), Phase VI (Wks 26-30 RTS)
Notes: Very conservative; submaximal isometrics only wks 14-18; maximal strength testing not until 10-12 months post-op

WB Status: FWB throughout; sling for support if recommended
Key Restrictions: Avoid heavy lifting, overhead activities, and pain-causing activities; progressive return to function
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 0-4 protection/pain management), Phase II (Wks 4-8 restoring ROM), Phase III (Wks 8-12 progressive strengthening), Phase IV (Wks 12+ return to function)
Notes: For patients not surgical candidates; deltoid-dominant compensatory approach; multidisciplinary team recommended

WB Status: Sling x3 weeks (off 4x/day); wean at wk 4
Key Restrictions: No ABD >90 deg wks 1-4; shoulder ER 0-40 deg wks 1-4; no lifting >1lb wks 1-4; no lifting >2lbs until wk 6; no AROM until wk 6; no isotonic shoulder strengthening wks 4-8; no lifting >10lbs wks 8-12
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 1-4 initial ROM), Phase II (Wks 4-8 AAROM/AROM), Phase III (Wks 8-12 initial strengthening), Phase IV (Wks 12+ advanced strengthening)

WB Status: Sling day and night with abduction pillow (30-45 deg) wks 1-6; gradually removed wks 6-10 with surgeon clearance
Key Restrictions: Same restrictions as small-medium RCR; subscapularis repair: no ER >0 deg, no ABD >90 deg in Phase I; more cautious progression for multi-tendon tears; no empty can position ever; no straight arm lateral raise
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 1-6 immobilization), Phase II (Wks 6-10 PROM), Phase III (Wks 10-18 AAROM/AROM + isometrics wks 14-18), Phase IV (Wks 18-22 initial strengthening), Phase V (Wks 22-26 advanced strengthening), Phase VI (Wks 26-30 RTS)
Notes: Identical timeline to small-medium RCR but more conservative progression; concomitant subscapularis repair requires extra ROM caution in Phase I

WB Status: Sling in neutral with abduction pillow (30-45 deg ABD); sleep in sling; wean during day wks 4-6; D/C at wks 7-8
Key Restrictions: No AROM wks 0-3; no reaching behind back (especially into IR); no excessive ER or ABD; no lifting >coffee cup wks 4-8; no lifting >10lbs wks 7-11; avoid ER above 80 deg ABD; no WB through hands
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 0-3 immediate), Phase II (Wks 4-6 intermediate), Phase III (Wks 7-8), Phase IV (Wks 9-11 transitional), Phase V (Wks 12-16 advanced strengthening)
Notes: Covers both TSA and hemiarthroplasty; factors include surgical approach, concurrent RTC repair, and reason for arthroplasty

WB Status: Sling in neutral with abduction pillow (30-45 deg); sleep in sling; D/C at wks 7-8
Key Restrictions: No AROM/AAROM wks 0-6; no elbow AROM (avoid biceps contraction) wks 0-8; no lifting wks 0-8; no reaching behind back; no resisted elbow flexion wks 7-12; no lifting >10lbs wks 7-12
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 0-3 immediate), Phase II (Wks 4-6 intermediate), Phase III (Wks 7-8), Phase IV (Wks 9-12 transitional), Phase V (Wks 13-16 late), Phase VI (Months 4-6 early RTS with throwing program)
Notes: Biceps contraction must be avoided until wk 8; ER at 90 deg ABD should not exceed 90 deg

WB Status: Sling x6 weeks; no WB through involved extremity; no lifting wks 0-6; no lifting >5lbs until wk 9; no lifting restrictions at ~4 months
Key Restrictions: No PROM GH joint wks 0-6; no scapular AROM (protraction/retraction/depression/elevation) wks 0-6; no running/jumping wks 0-6; no lifting >5lbs until wk 9; no repetitive overhead activities wks 6-12
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 0-6 early post-op), Phase II (Wks 6-12 intermediate), Phase III (Wks 13+ late post-op/return to sport)
Notes: Tendon autograft repair; scapular AROM restricted wks 0-6 to protect reconstruction site

WB Status: Sling day and night x4 weeks; discontinue sling wks 4-8
Key Restrictions: Shoulder ER limited to 30 deg wks 0-4; shoulder IR limited to belt line wks 0-4; shoulder ABD limited to 90 deg wks 0-4; no AROM wks 0-4; no lifting/WB wks 0-4; no shoulder ER at 90 deg ABD wks 4-8; avoid lifting/resisted exercises wks 4-8; no forceful ER stretching wks 8+
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 0-4 PROM/protection), Phase II (Wks 4-8 AAROM/AROM), Phase III (Wks 8-12 initial strengthening), Phase IV (Wks 12-16 progressive strengthening), Phase V (Wks 16-20 advanced strengthening)

WB Status: FWB throughout; avoid activities that increase intra-abdominal pressure
Key Restrictions: Avoid lifting or activities increasing intra-abdominal pressure throughout; no running until pain-free jogging; hip (flexion/abduction/adduction) LSI <20% before return to running
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Acute - pain control/ROM/isometrics), Phase II (Subacute - PRE/return to running), Phase III (Early RTS - plyometrics/sprinting/agility), Phase IV (Unrestricted RTS)
Notes: Criterion-based; covers adductor/pubic/inguinal/iliopsoas-related groin pain per DOHA 2015 terminology; HAGOS questionnaire >90% for RTS

WB Status: WBAT throughout
Key Restrictions: Limit hamstring stretching (trunk flexion, knee extension) wks 0-2; limit hip/knee ROM to avoid stretch/strain sensation; no jogging until wk 6 if full symmetrical ROM and strength; no sprinting until Phase V (wks 12+)
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 0-2 early), Phase II (Wks 2-4 intermediate), Phase III (Wks 4-8 transitional), Phase IV (Wks 8-12 early RTS), Phase V (Wks 12+ unrestricted RTS)
Notes: Criterion-based with time guidance; varies by severity, strain grade, and location (proximal/mid-belly/distal); proximal injuries may have concurrent sciatic nerve tension; LSI hamstring/glute/quad >95% for RTP

WB Status: FWB; walking from day 1 (15 min 2x/day easy pace)
Key Restrictions: Avoid lifting or activities that increase intra-abdominal pressure (Valsalva) throughout wks 0-8; no jogging until wk 6+ (pain-free); hip index (flexion/abduction/adduction/extension) <20% before return to jogging
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 0-1 immediate), Phase II (Wks 2-3 intermediate), Phase III (Wks 4-5 late post-op), Phase IV (Wks 6-8 transitional), Phase V (Wks 9-12 early RTS), Phase VI (Month 3+ unrestricted RTS)
Notes: Post-operative; avoid Valsalva throughout; HAGOS questionnaire >90% and hip index >=90% for RTS; adductor/abductor ratio >80% using HHD

WB Status: PWB 20lbs step-to pattern wks 0-3; gradually increase to WBAT wks 4-6; wean from crutches by 6-8 weeks
Key Restrictions: Hip flex 0-90 deg, ext to neutral, ABD 0-30 deg, ER/IR 0-30 deg wks 0-3; no active SLR wks 0-8; no active hip flexion days 0-21; no Grade III-IV hip mobilization wks 0-6; no long axis distraction wks 0-8 (labral repair) or wks 0-12 (labral reconstruction); no sitting >30 min first 2 wks; no extreme combined ROM (flexion/IR or flexion/ER); no plyometrics or running until month 4+
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 0-3 immediate), Phase II (Wks 4-6 intermediate), Phase III (Wks 7-12 late), Phase IV (Wks 12+ transitional), Phase V (Month 4+ early RTS)
Notes: Capsular closure requires limiting hip ER/extension early; labral repair vs reconstruction affects long axis distraction restriction (8 wks vs 12 wks); jogging initiated at 16-18 weeks per surgeon; outcome measure: Hip Outcome Score (HOS)

WB Status: TDWB with crutches wks 0-2; PWB 50% with crutches wks 2-6; progressively wean to FWB wks 6-12
Key Restrictions: Hip brace limiting flex to 45 deg wks 0-2; brace hip flex limit 60 deg wks 2-6 (gradually to 90 deg by wk 6); avoid hip flexion with knee extension; no active hamstrings or hip extension exercises wks 0-6; no running until wks 16-20; no sprinting or jump training until wks 20-22+; discontinue brace at 6-8 wks per MD
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 0-2 immediate), Phase II (Wks 2-6 intermediate), Phase III (Wks 6-12 late), Phase IV (Wks 13-16 transitional), Phase V (Wks 16-20 early RTS), Phase VI (Wks 20-24 unrestricted RTS)
Notes: Hip brace required wks 0-6; outcomes affected by duration before surgery, tendon retraction, and number of tendons repaired; jump training not until wk 22; LSI hamstring/glute/quad >95% for RTP

WB Status: FWB; non-weight bearing on involved upper extremity during exercise
Key Restrictions: Avoid aggravating activities; activity modification throughout; avoid post-exercise pain/swelling; 70% strength contralateral side before Phase III; 90% strength contralateral side for RTS
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 0-2 acute), Phase II (Wks 2-4 intermediate/sub-acute), Phase III (Wks 4-6+ late/chronic)
Notes: Non-operative; covers both medial (FCR/flexor tendons) and lateral (ECRB/extensor tendons); outcome measures: PRTEE, DASH

WB Status: Non-weight bearing on repaired upper extremity; hinged brace wks 1-6 (D/C at wk 6 if full ROM)
Key Restrictions: No active elbow flexion or forearm supination until wk 4; no lifting until wk 8; no valgus stress to graft; no ER strengthening until wk 6; no throwing until wk 16+; gracilis graft: no progressive hamstring strengthening until wk 6; interval throwing program at wk 16
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 0-3 immediate), Phase II (Wks 4-6 protection), Phase III (Wks 6-12 strengthening), Phase IV (Wks 12-16 advanced strengthening), Phase V (Wks 16+ early/unrestricted RTS)
Notes: Palmaris longus graft: wrist compression 5-7 days; gracilis graft: knee ROM immediately/bike at wk 3; KJOC throwers assessment for RTS

WB Status: Non-weight bearing on repaired UE; posterior orthosis at 90 deg days 0-5-7; hinged brace progressively unlocking wks 2-6; begin gradual WB at wk 8 (elbow flexed), extended elbow by wk 10
Key Restrictions: No active elbow flexion or forearm supination until wk 4; no lifting until wk 8; submaximal isometrics at wk 10; bilateral plyometrics after wk 16; single arm plyometrics after wks 20-22
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Days 0-1 immediate), Phase II (Wks 2-6 intermediate), Phase III (Wks 7-10 late), Phase IV (Wks 11-15 transitional), Phase V (Months 4-6 early RTS), Phase VI (Months 6+ unrestricted RTS)
Notes: Hinged brace extension progressively unlocked wks 2-6 (90 deg → 45 → 30 → 20 → D/C); outcomes affected by suture materials; KJOC assessment for discharge

WB Status: NWB in splint/cast wks 0-2; partial progressive WB in boot wks 2-4; FWB in boot wks 4-6 (no SPR repair) or wk 6+ (SPR repaired); wean boot at wk 6 (no SPR) or wk 8 (SPR repaired)
Key Restrictions: If SPR repaired: no inversion/eversion until wk 6; if SPR repaired: no isometric eversion until wk 6; no running until wks 12-16+; LE functional tests >=90% for RTS
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 0-2 immediate), Phase II (Wks 2-4 intermediate), Phase III (Wks 4-8 late), Phase IV (Wks 9-12 transitional), Phase V (Wks 12-16 advanced), Phase VI (Months 6+ RTS)
Notes: SPR repair status dictates inversion/eversion and eversion strengthening timeline; outcomes affected by tendoscopic vs open approach; PRRS for RTS

WB Status: NWB in splint/Achilles boot wks 0-3; PWB with 3 wedges starting wk 4 (25% BW/week progression); wean one wedge/week (wks 5, 6, 7); FWB boot no wedges wk 8; sneaker with 1cm heel lift wks 9-10; wean heel lift wks 11-12
Key Restrictions: Do not dorsiflex past 0 deg until wk 8; no overt calf stretching until 6 months post-op; no IASTM directly on tendon until wk 16; no running until months 6+; LE functional tests >=90% for RTS
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 0-3 immediate), Phase II (Wks 4-6 intermediate), Phase III (Wks 7-8 late), Phase IV (Wks 9-10 transitional), Phase V (Wks 11-12 transitional), Phase VI (Months 3-6 advanced), Phase VII (Months 6+ RTS)
Notes: More conservative for re-rupture, revision, tendon augmentation, chronic tendinosis, or comorbidities; Shoe Leveler for uninvolved side throughout boot phase; outcome measures: ATRS, PRRS

WB Status: WBAT from day 1 with brace/tape; immobilization recommended for 10 days for severe sprain
Key Restrictions: Brace or protective tape during WB activities; immobilization up to 10 days for severe sprain; 25 single leg heel raises before Phase III; 90% Y-balance/hop tests for RTS
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 1-2 protection/optimal loading), Phase II (Wks 3-6 intermediate/sub-acute), Phase III (Wks 7-10 late/chronic), Phase IV (Wks 11-16 RTS/functional activities)
Notes: Non-operative lateral ankle sprain; Ottawa ankle rule to rule out fracture; outcome measures: Cumberland Ankle Instability Tool (CAIT) or FAAM

WB Status: NWB with crutches wks 0-2; WBAT in boot wks 2-8; WBAT in shoe with active ankle brace wks 7-8+; FWB in shoe wks 9+
Key Restrictions: No inversion or plantarflexion ROM wks 0-2; no inversion throughout early phases; ankle mobilizations must avoid stress to CFL and ATFL; 90% Y-balance/hop tests and CAIT/FAAM for RTS
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 0-2 immediate), Phase II (Wks 2-8 intermediate), Phase III (Wks 7-8 late), Phase IV (Wks 9-12 transitional), Phase V (Months 3-4 early RTS), Phase VI (Months 5-6+ unrestricted RTS)
Notes: More cautious for tendon augmentation, peroneal repair, revision, hyperlaxity, or comorbidities; avoid stress to CFL and ATFL with mobilizations; outcome measures: CAIT or FAAM

WB Status: NWB in splint wks 0-2; standing WB in boot only (NWB walking) wks 3-5; WBAT in boot wk 6; wean boot wks 7-8; FWB in sneaker wk 8+
Key Restrictions: Elevate ankle above heart level majority of day wks 0-2; if tendon transfers: no resistance strengthening or stretching of involved musculature until 3 months post-op; no high-impact activities ever; swelling may persist 6-12 months
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 0-2 immediate), Phase II (Wks 3-5 intermediate), Phase III (Wks 6-10 late), Phase IV (Wks 11-13 transitional), Phase V (Wks 14-16 transitional), Phase VI (Months 4-6 advanced), Phase VII (Months 6+ low-impact RTS)
Notes: Reserved for older adults with lower activity demands; ROM improvement may vary; no return to high-impact sport ever; outcome measures: Y-Balance, Star Excursion Balance Test, PRRS

WB Status: FWB; sling worn at all times wks 0-3 for GH joint support
Key Restrictions: ROM wks 0-3: 90° flex, 90° abd, 0° ER, 0° ext; avoid elbow hyperextension; ROM wks 3-6: 120° flex/abd, 30° ER; no active arm swing until wk 4; ROM wks 6-12: 60° ER; strength 95% uninvolved for RTS
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 0-3), Phase II (Wks 3-6), Phase III (Wks 6-8), Phase IV (Wks 8-12), Phase V (Wks 12-24 RTS)
Notes: RTS goal by 24 weeks; strength 95% uninvolved side

WB Status: FWB; sling for comfort wk 0-1
Key Restrictions: Avoid active elevation (anterior deltoid) until wk 6; no resistive biceps until wk 6 if concurrent biceps tenodesis; RTS criteria: MD clearance, full pain-free ROM, 85% strength uninvolved, completion of RTS program
Timeline / Phases: 0-1 wk (sling/comfort, pendulums), 1-2 wks (PROM/isometrics), 2-6 wks (progress ROM/strengthening), 6-12 wks (independent program)
Notes: Also covers arthroscopic AC excision and partial-thickness RCT debridement; 85% strength RTS criterion

WB Status: FWB; sling/swathe wks 0-2 at all times (except bathing); sling required at school/crowds/car/sleep wks 2-6; discontinued wks 6-12
Key Restrictions: ROM wks 0-2: 90° flex, 45° abd, 0° ER, 20° ext; ROM wks 2-6: 90° flex, 90° abd, 30° ER, 20° ext; ROM wks 6-12: 60° ER, full flex/abd/ext; throwing/contact RTS at 9 months per strength and physician assessment
Timeline / Phases: 0-2 wks (immobilization), 2-6 wks (PROM/AAROM within limits), 6-12 wks (progress PROM/AROM, strengthening), 12-24 wks (independent program, sport-specific training), 9 months (throwing/contact RTS)
Notes: 9 months for full throwing and contact sport; physician assessment required for RTS

WB Status: FWB; sling 3-6 weeks per MD; may do bike/walking with sling on
Key Restrictions: Avoid biceps tension for 6 weeks; limit ER to 40° and avoid extension/horizontal abduction past neutral for 4 weeks; avoid heavy load/long lever activation wks 6-8; avoid forearm supination with biceps activation; no throwing/swimming/sports until wks 8-12+; RTS cleared by surgeon and PT
Timeline / Phases: 0-6 wks (protection, sling per MD), 6-8 wks (gradual bicep resistance), 8-12 wks (strengthening, no sport), 12-20 wks (low-velocity sport-prep), 20+ wks (sport-specific/RTS)
Notes: With/without subacromial decompression and/or RTC debridement; avoid forearm supination when activating biceps in early phases; RTS cleared by ortho surgeon and PT

WB Status: FWB; sling at all times wks 0-2; sling at work/school/crowds/car/sleep wks 2-4; sling in crowds/sleep wks 4-6; discontinued wks 6-12
Key Restrictions: ROM wks 0-2: 90° flex, 45° abd, 0° ER, 20° ext, 70° IR; ROM wks 2-4: 120° flex, 90° abd, 30° ER, no IR behind back; ROM wks 4-6: 145° abd, 30° ER, no IR behind back; ROM wks 6-12: full flex/abd/ext, 60° ER; noncontact RTS at 24 wks; throwing/contact at 9 months
Timeline / Phases: 0-2 wks, 2-4 wks, 4-6 wks, 6-12 wks, 12-24 wks (independent program), 24 wks (noncontact/throwing RTS), 9 months (contact RTS)
Notes: No IR behind back until wk 6+; noncontact/throwing RTS at 24 weeks; contact sport at 9 months per strength and physician assessment

WB Status: FWB; immobilizer day and night wks 0-2; immobilizer daytime only wks 2-6; no immobilizer from wk 6
Key Restrictions: No PROM wks 0-3; PROM limits wks 3-6: 90° flex, 45° ER, 20° ext, 45° abd; no resisted IR/adduction until Phase III; avoid anterior capsule stretch; keep strengthening below horizontal plane Phase II; no plank/push-ups until wk 16; limited RTS at 4-5 months
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (0-6 wks, immobilizer), Phase II (6-12 wks, no resisted IR/adduction), Phase III (~12-16 wks, eccentrics/plyometrics), Phase IV (4-5 months, limited RTS), Phase V (full RTS)
Notes: No PROM for first 3 weeks; no resisted IR/adduction in Phase II; cycling/running at 12 weeks; limited return to sports at 4-5 months

WB Status: FWB; UltraSling 4-6 weeks (4 wks: traumatic/acute; 6 wks: atraumatic/ligamentous laxity)
Key Restrictions: Protect posterior capsule — avoid extremes of IR throughout; PROM restricted: 90° FF and ER to 0° by 4 wks postop; isokinetic testing at 6 months (≥80% strength for sports-specific program); RTS: 12 months throwing, 9-12 months power/contact, 8-12 months non-contact
Timeline / Phases: 4-6 wks (discontinue sling, PROM/AAROM/isometrics), 6-8 wks (advance ROM, active ER), 2-3 months (full ROM, isotonics), 4 months (eccentric RC, isokinetics), 5 months (advance), 6 months (isokinetic testing, sports-specific)
Notes: Immobilization duration based on injury type; ≥80% isokinetic strength at 6 months before sport-specific program; 9-12 month RTS for contact/power athletes

WB Status: FWB; hold formal PT and wear sling for 4 weeks; home pendulums/elbow AROM from Day 1
Key Restrictions: Hold formal PT 0-4 weeks; sling 4 weeks; ROM limits wks 4-6: flex 0-150°, ER 30°, abd 90°; no IR resistance until >6 wks; discharge when ROM plateau reached
Timeline / Phases: 0-4 wks (sling, hold PT, home pendulums/AROM only), 4-6 wks (PROM/AAROM within limits, isometrics except IR), >6 wks (Thera-Band, scapular stabilization, add IR)
Notes: Dr. Murray's protocol specifically; home pendulums and elbow/wrist AROM only for first 4 weeks (no formal PT); discharge to independent program when ROM plateau reached

WB Status: FWB; ROM limits 0-6 wks: flex 0-150°, ER 30°, abd 90°
Key Restrictions: ROM limits 0-6 wks: flex 0-150°, ER 30°, abd 90°; no IR resistance until >6 wks; discharge to independent program when ROM plateau reached
Timeline / Phases: 0-6 wks (PROM/AAROM within limits, isometrics except IR, begin Day 1-2), >6 wks (Thera-Band, scapular stabilization, add IR)
Notes: Dr. Wolf's protocol; PT begins Day 1-2 unlike Dr. Murray's 4-week hold; discharge when ROM plateau reached

WB Status: FWB; sling at all times wks 0-3
Key Restrictions: ROM wks 0-3: 90° flex, 90° abd, 0° ER, 0° ext; avoid elbow hyperextension; ROM wks 3-6: 120° flex/abd, 30° ER; no active arm swing until wk 4; ROM wks 6-12: 60° ER; strength 95% uninvolved for RTS by 24 weeks
Timeline / Phases: 0-3 wks, 3-6 wks, 6-8 wks, 8-12 wks (ROM goal: full PROM ER to 60° by wk 12), 12-24 wks (independent program/sport-specific)
Notes: RTS goal by 24 weeks; strength 95% uninvolved side

WB Status: FWB; sling/swathe at all times wks 0-6; sling/swathe except exercises/bathing/sitting wks 6-8; sling for comfort (may discontinue) wks 8-10
Key Restrictions: Maximum protection 0-6 weeks; PROM limits wks 6-8: 90° abd, 20° ext, 70° IR (not behind back); AROM initiated wks 10-12; no overhead strengthening until surgeon clearance (wks 12-20+); no overhead sport or heavy lifting until wks 20-24+; indications: massive irreparable RCT, intact deltoid/subscapularis/nerve function
Timeline / Phases: 0-6 wks (max protection), 6-8 wks (PROM within limits), 8-10 wks (AAROM, sling comfort), 10-12 wks (AROM, isometrics), 12-20+ wks (resisted strength, surgeon clearance), 20-24+ wks (advanced strength, sport-specific)
Notes: For massive irreparable RCT without arthritis; requires intact deltoid and subscapularis; no overhead sport or heavy lifting through advanced phase; surgeon clearance required for resistive and advanced training phases

WB Status: FWB; sling full time wks 0-4 (except bathing/exercises); sling for comfort wks 4-6 or in uncontrolled situations
Key Restrictions: Sling full time until wk 4; sling for comfort wks 4-6; no shoulder flexion isometrics wks 2-6; no UBE until wk 4; goal full AROM without shoulder hiking by 12 weeks
Timeline / Phases: 0-2 wks (sling/PROM/AAROM, pendulums), 2-6 wks (AAROM/AROM, isometrics except flexion), 6-12 wks (AROM, strengthening, independent home program)
Notes: Open and arthroscopic small tear; goal full AROM without shoulder hiking by 12 weeks

WB Status: FWB; ROM limits wks 0-3: ER to 45°, flexion to 90°
Key Restrictions: ROM limits wks 0-3: ER 45°, flexion 90°; discharge to independent program when ROM plateau reached
Timeline / Phases: 0-3 wks (PROM/AAROM within limits, pendulums from Day 1), 3-4 wks (AAROM/AROM, isometrics, scapular), 4-6 wks (Thera-Band, postural education)
Notes: Also covers hemiarthroplasty with intact rotator cuff; discharge to independent program when ROM plateau reached

WB Status: FWB; sling full time wks 0-4 (except bathing/exercises); sling for comfort daytime wks 4-6; wear sling at night through wk 6
Key Restrictions: PROM limits wks 0-2: 90° pure abd, 20° ext, 70° IR (not behind back); subscapularis repair: limit ER to 0° (wks 0-2), 30° (wks 2-6), progress 10°/week from wk 6; no ROM limits from wk 6; goal full AROM by 12 weeks
Timeline / Phases: 0-2 wks, 2-6 wks, 6-12 wks (no ROM limits, AAROM/AROM), 12-18 wks (resistive exercises)
Notes: Open and arthroscopic medium tear; subscapularis repair: ER limit 0° → 30° → 10°/week progression from wk 6; goal full AROM by 12 weeks

WB Status: FWB; sling/swathe at all times wks 0-6 (except bathing/sitting from wk 2); sling for comfort wks 6-12 (may discontinue)
Key Restrictions: PROM limits wks 0-6: 90° pure abd, 20° ext, 70° IR (not behind back); avoid subscapularis activation until 4-6 wks; no reaching behind back until wk 8+; patient must elevate without shoulder hiking before progressing; resistive exercises per surgeon wks 12-18
Timeline / Phases: 0-2 wks, 2-6 wks, 6-12 wks (AAROM/AROM, sling for comfort), 12-18 wks (resistive exercises per surgeon)
Notes: Open and arthroscopic large tear; resistive exercises held per surgeon from wks 12-18; no shoulder hiking is criterion for progression

WB Status: WBAT; crutch-assisted until minimal limp; brace 1-2 wks
Key Restrictions: No resisted leg extension machine at any point; if hamstring graft: no isolated hamstring until 3 wks, no open chain resisted hamstring curls until at least 6 wks; ROM 0-90° by 2 wks, 0-115° by 4 wks
Notes: Preoperative PT visit; functional assessment at 14 weeks (ground clock, unilateral squat, lateral shuffle, jump mechanics); jogging at 12-14 weeks with good quad control

WB Status: NWB 3-4 wks; knee immobilizer 4 wks
Key Restrictions: NWB 3-4 wks; knee immobilizer 4 wks; CKC between 20-70° or full extension only; avoid submaximal CKC 8 wks; active hamstring at 6 wks, resistive at 8 wks; no lateral exercises 10 wks; no pivoting/ballistic ≥4 months; no resisted leg extension at any point
Notes: Functional assessments at 14 weeks and 5-6 months postop

WB Status: TDWB 3-5 days, then FWB in extension; knee immobilizer 3 wks
Key Restrictions: Knee immobilizer 3 wks; TDWB 3-5 days; CKC 20-70° or full extension only; avoid submaximal CKC 6 wks; active hamstring at 4 wks, resistive at 6 wks; no lateral exercises 6-8 wks; no pivoting/ballistic ≥4 months; no resisted leg extension at any point
Notes: Watch for lower leg rotation or heel whip with ambulation; functional assessments at 14 weeks and 5-6 months

WB Status: TDWB 4-6 wks; knee immobilizer 4 wks
Key Restrictions: Knee immobilizer 4 wks; TDWB 4-6 wks; CKC 20-70° or full extension only; avoid submaximal CKC 8 wks; active hamstring at 6 wks, resistive at 8 wks; no lateral exercises 10 wks; no pivoting/ballistic ≥4 months; no resisted leg extension at any point
Notes: Cybex/functional assessment at 14 weeks, 6 months, and 1 year postop

WB Status: TDWB 4 wks; PWB 4-6 wks
Key Restrictions: Knee immobilizer 0-1 wk; long hinge brace 0-30° wks 1-2, increase 10°/wk to 90°, continue 0-90° until 6 wks; ROM restricted 0-70° for 4 wks; no resisted hamstring 4 wks; CPM 6 hrs/day x 4 wks (0-70°); no resisted leg extension at any point; no high-impact/cutting/twisting ≥4 months
Notes: CPM 6 hours/day for 4 weeks; MD visits at day 1, 8-10, 1 month, 2 months, 4 months, 6 months, and 1 year postop

WB Status: WBAT; knee immobilizer for ambulation 2 wks; FWB by end of wk 2
Key Restrictions: Knee immobilizer for ambulation 2 wks; regular patellar mobilization (grade I-II if good mobility ≥3 quadrants); RTS: involved limb deficiencies <10% uninvolved, surgeon clearance (usually 6-9 months)
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Day 1 to 6 wks, OKC focus), Phase II (Wks 7-12, CKC/lateral), Phase III (Wks 13-16, plyometrics), Phase IV (Wks 17+ RTS)
Notes: For extensor mechanism realignment with MPFL reconstruction, use Tibial Tubercle Osteotomy protocol; RTS usually 6-9 months per surgeon clearance

Surgery Type: with Medial and Lateral Retinaculum Repair
WB Status: WBAT; brace locked at 0° for 4 wks
Key Restrictions: ROM blocked at 0° for 4 wks; no flexion past 90° for 8 wks; brace 0-40° wks 4-6; brace 0-80° wks 6-8; RTS dependent on functional sports test
Notes: Return to sports dependent on passing a functional sports test

WB Status: TDWB with crutches 4 wks; hinged neoprene brace 8 wks (locked full extension 4 wks, then 0-30° progressing 15°/wk)
Key Restrictions: TDWB 4 wks; hinged brace locked at full extension 4 wks; no resisted knee flexion 8 wks (passive flexion OK); wean brace 8-12 wks
Notes: Light jog possible at 4 months, agilities at 5 months, sport-specific drills at 6 months

WB Status: WBAT; ROM blocked at 0° for 2 wks; brace 0-40° wks 4-6 per MD
Key Restrictions: ROM blocked at 0° for 2 wks; no flexion past 90° for 6-8 wks; brace 0-40° wks 4-6; brace 0-80° wks 6-8; brace unlocked by 8 wks if quad control good; RTS dependent on functional sports test
Notes: Return to sports dependent on passing a functional sports test

WB Status: FWB in extension 4 wks; knee immobilizer 3 wks
Key Restrictions: Knee immobilizer 3 wks; FWB in extension only 4 wks; CKC 20-90° or full extension; avoid submaximal CKC 6 wks; active hamstring at 4 wks, resistive at 6 wks; no lateral exercises 6-8 wks; no pivoting/ballistic ≥4 months; no resisted leg extension at any point
Notes: Cybex/functional assessment at 14 weeks, 6 months, and 1 year postop

WB Status: TDWB with crutches and immobilizer (blocked 0°) 4 wks; WBAT with crutches wks 4-8
Key Restrictions: TDWB with immobilizer blocked at 0° for 4 wks; no flexion past 90° for 4 wks; avoid active knee extension wks 4-6; regular patellar mobilization (superior and inferior only); FWB by wk 8; RTS dependent on functional sports test and surgeon clearance
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Day 1-4 wks), Phase II (Wks 4-8), Phase III (Wks 8-12), Phase IV (Wks 12-16), Phase V (Wks 16-6 months), Phase VI (6+ months RTS)
Notes: Also covers extensor mechanism realignment with MPFL reconstruction; RTS dependent on functional sports assessment and surgeon clearance

WB Status: WBAT; walker/crutches/cane until cleared by MD/PT; goal: no assistive device by 6 wks
Key Restrictions: ROM goals: 0-90° by 2 wks, 0-100° by 6 wks, 0-120° by 12 wks; compression stockings 2 wks; driving cleared by MD usually 4-6 wks; ice 3-4x/day 15-20 min; change knee position ≥1x/hr when awake
Timeline / Phases: 0-2 wks (ROM 0-90°, WBAT), 2-6 wks (ROM 0-100°, CKC, bike), 6-12 wks (ROM 0-120°, single-leg exercises, stairs), 12+ wks (return to activities)
Notes: Pre-operative PT visit for gait training and home exercise instruction; compression stockings 2 weeks; home exercises 3x daily

WB Status: WBAT with brace; crutches to assist; brace worn while sleeping 4 wks
Key Restrictions: Brace while sleeping 4 wks; ROM 0-90° x4 wks; discontinue brace at 4-6 wks; ROM goals: 0-110° by wks 4-6, 0-130° by wks 6-8, full ROM by wks 8-12; return to sport >6 months with MD approval and functional test
Timeline / Phases: Wk 1, Wks 2-4, 4-6 (ROM 0-110°), 6-8 (ROM 0-130°), 8-12 (full ROM), 12+ (sport-specific, sports test at 3/6/9 months)
Notes: Functional assessment/sports test at 3, 6, and 9 months; no fins swimming until >12 weeks; return to sport >6 months if approved by MD and passing sports functional test

WB Status: NWB 4-6 wks (per MD); TDWB in full extension may be allowed per MD; WBAT wks 7-9
Key Restrictions: NWB 4-6 wks; immobilizer locked at 0° until 4 wks; CKC at 6 wks in full extension or 20-70° only (maintained until 4 months); CKC limited to 90° until 20 wks; avoid deep knee flexion CKC 6 months; active hamstring at 6 wks, resistive at 8-10 wks; avoid pivoting/twisting; quad strength 80% uninvolved before plyometrics, 90% for RTS
Timeline / Phases: Wks 1-6 (NWB/TDWB, ROM 0-120° by wk 6), Wks 7-9 (WBAT, normalize gait), Wks 10-15 (strength/endurance, CKC <70°), Wks 16-20 (dynamic SL, plyometrics), Wk 20+ (return to running, RTS at 6 months)
Notes: Deep squatting past parallel avoided until 6 months; RTS transition begins at 6 months if passing functional evaluation (Vail Sport Test, hop testing battery, 90% quad symmetry); return to sport per functional exam and MD evaluation

WB Status: FWB; elbow brace blocked at 45° wks 2-3
Key Restrictions: Brace blocked at 45° (wk 3: 30°) to full flexion wks 2-3; AROM extension only to -45° wks 2-3; passive supination with elbow at 90° only wks 2-3; discharge brace at wk 4 except at-risk situations; no progressive resisted exercise until wk 8; return to sport-specific at 12-14 wks
Timeline / Phases: Wks 2-3 (brace 45°→full flex, PROM, submaximal isometrics), Wk 4 (discharge brace, AAROM), Wk 5 (AROM all planes), Wks 6-8 (combined motions), Wk 8 (progressive resisted exercises), Wks 12-14 (sport-specific, throwing return)
Notes: ROM progression may be adjusted per surgeon assessment; throwing athletes can start return to throwing at 12-14 weeks; authored by Drs. Hoffman, Murray, and Wolf

WB Status: FWB; tip protector splint continuously until 6 wks
Key Restrictions: No PROM and no strength testing at initial evaluation; tip protector splint continuously with pin protected; pin removed 4-6 wks; mallet finger splint from 6 wks (continuous except HEP); PROM DIP begins at 7 wks only if no extensor lag; wean from mallet splint starting 9 wks (reduce 1 hr/day); discontinue splint at night 12 wks; monitor for extensor lag throughout
Timeline / Phases: 3-5 days (tip protector splint, A/PROM MP/PIP), 4-6 wks (pin removal), 6 wks (AROM DIP 6x/day, mallet splint), 7 wks (PROM DIP if no lag), 9 wks (wean splint), 12 wks (discontinue night splint)
Notes: No PROM or strength testing at initial evaluation; continuously monitor for extensor lag throughout course of therapy

WB Status: FWB; short arm thumb spica splint from 2 wks
Key Restrictions: Post-op dressing 0-2 wks; thumb spica splint from 2 wks (except showering); no lifting until 6 wks; therapy starts at 6 wks; avoid strengthening thumb adductor; light strengthening at 8-10 wks; unrestricted use at 12 wks
Timeline / Phases: 0-2 wks (post-op dressing), 2 wks (thumb spica splint), 6 wks (wean splint, therapy, A/PROM, scar massage), 8-10 wks (light strengthening), 12 wks (unrestricted use)
Notes: FCR stays intact; strengthening focus: 1st dorsal interossei, APB and APL (avoid thumb adductor; avoid MP hyperextension); Revised 6/2020

WB Status: FWB; soft wrist splint at night 2-3 wks initially
Key Restrictions: Therapy begins 4-6 wks post injury/surgery; no strengthening (including grip) until 9-12 wks; soft wrist splint at night initially; wrist extension and supination are priority ROM; grip/pinch strengthening at 9-12 wks only if wrist flex/ext at 50-55°; splint discontinued by 12-15 wks
Timeline / Phases: 4-6 wks (cast/fixator removed, A/PROM, no strengthening), 7-8 wks (scar massage, wean splint), 9-12 wks (grip/pinch and wrist strengthening), 12-15 wks (discontinue splint, continue strengthening)
Notes: Covers post-surgical and post-injury management; if PROM not returning for wrist extension/supination, consider dynamic splinting (confirm no bony blocks first); if concurrent carpal tunnel release, avoid repetitive motion until symptoms resolved

WB Status: FWB; thumb spica splint continuously until 7 wks
Key Restrictions: No PROM and no strength testing at initial evaluation; thumb spica (thumb palmar/radial abduction, IF slight hyperextension, wrist 20° extension) continuously 3-5 days through 6 wks; AROM only from 4-6 wks; PROM at 6 wks; wean splint at 6 wks (reduce 1 hr/day); discontinue splint at 7 wks; monitor for extensor lag throughout
Timeline / Phases: 3-5 days (thumb spica splint continuous), 4-6 wks (AROM thumb IP ext, splint continuous except exercises), 6 wks (PROM, wean splint), 7 wks (discontinue splint, dynamic flexion, putty strengthening)
Notes: No PROM or strength testing at initial evaluation; monitor for extensor lag during splint weaning; EPL is the extensor pollicis longus (thumb extensor)

WB Status: FWB; volar orthosis continuously except during exercises
Key Restrictions: Volar orthosis (PIP and DIP at 0°) continuously except exercises; two template orthoses for SAM protocol (Template 1: PIP 30°/DIP 20-25° flex; Template 2: PIP 0°); exercises hourly, 20 reps per template, wrist in 30° flex, hold extension 5 sec; progress PIP flex at 2 wks (30°→40°) and 4 wks (40°→50°) if no lag; discontinue orthosis at 6 wks; light activities at 8 wks; monitor for extension lag throughout
Timeline / Phases: 3-5 days (volar orthosis, hourly SAM exercises), 2 wks (adjust to 40° PIP flex if no lag), 4 wks (adjust to 50° PIP flex if no lag), 6 wks (discontinue orthosis, strengthening), 8 wks (light activities, progress strengthening)
Notes: Early Active Short Arc Motion (SAM) Protocol per Evans and Thompson; if lateral bands repaired, limit DIP flexion to 30-35 degrees; monitor for extension lag throughout all stages

WB Status: FWB; volar wrist orthosis (30° wrist ext) continuously except exercises
Key Restrictions: No PROM and no strength testing at initial evaluation; volar wrist orthosis (30° wrist ext, MCP 0°) continuously except exercises; no composite flexion until 6-10 wks; protected PROM in-therapy only (3-5 days); AROM tenodesis at 3 wks; composite MP/IP flexion at 4 wks; orthosis only as needed 6-10 wks (if no extensor lag); resistance strengthening at 10-12 wks
Timeline / Phases: 3-5 days (orthosis, protected PROM in therapy only), 3 wks (AROM tenodesis), 4 wks (composite MP/IP flex, monitor lag), 6-10 wks (orthosis as needed, composite flex if no lag, light strengthening), 10-12 wks (resistance strengthening)
Notes: No PROM or strength testing at initial evaluation; no composite flexion until 6-10 weeks; IP joint inclusion dependent on repair site relative to juncturae tendinum; monitor for extensor lag throughout

WB Status: FWB; dorsal protective splint at all times including sleep
Key Restrictions: Dorsal splint (wrist 30-40° flex, MP 60° flex, IP 0°) at all times including sleep; rubber band traction for passive flexion; active extension 10x/hr; place and hold at 3-4 wks; protect until 6 wks; discharge splint at 6 wks with wristlet; tendon blocking/gliding at 6 wks; no composite wrist+finger extension at 6 wks; no resistive activities until 12 wks if good active motion; no precautions at 12 wks
Timeline / Phases: Days 2-5 (rubber band traction, active ext), Wks 1-2, Wks 3-4 (place and hold), Wk 4, Wk 6 (discharge splint, tendon blocking), Wks 7-8, Wk 12 (no precautions)
Notes: If digital nerve injury, delay exercise progression to 3-4 wks; FPL variant: wrist 20° flex, MP 15° flex, IP 15° flex

WB Status: FWB; two alternating static orthoses at all times including shower
Key Restrictions: Two orthoses alternating daily (extension splint: wrist 30° ext, MCP/IP 0°; flexion splint: wrist 30° ext, MCP 70° flex, IP 0°); hourly exercises; joint protection principles introduced at wk 4; remove for light ADLs 3-4x/day in 30-min intervals at wks 6-8; discontinue day orthosis at 10-12 wks per surgeon; night orthosis worn for 1 YEAR; maintain neutral or slight radial deviation throughout
Timeline / Phases: Day 5-7 (two alternating orthoses, hourly exercises), Wk 4 (joint protection), Wks 6-8 (remove for light ADLs 30 min intervals), Wks 10-12 (DC day orthosis per surgeon)
Notes: Night orthosis required for 1 full year postop; maintain neutral/slight radial deviation during all exercises

WB Status: FWB; therapy begins at 6 wks postop
Key Restrictions: Thumb spica splint between sessions from 6 wks (remove for shower only); avoid radial/ulnar deviation until 8-10 wks; strengthening at 10 wks if motion is adequate
Timeline / Phases: 6 wks (A/PROM flex/ext, thumb spica between sessions), 8-10 wks (AROM deviation, dart-throwers motion), 10 wks (strengthening if motion adequate)
Notes: Uses Palmaris Longus-Arthrex system; F-659 Revised 6/2020

WB Status: FWB; thumb spica splint wks 0-6
Key Restrictions: Thumb spica cast/splint wks 0-2 (bag for shower); removable thumb spica wks 2-6; no pinching, grabbing, or lifting until wks 6-8; discontinue brace at 6-8 wks; avoid sustained heavy pinch until 16 wks; return to sport at 16 wks
Timeline / Phases: Wks 0-2 (thumb spica, ROM of uninvolved joints), Wks 2-4, Wks 4-6, Wks 6-8 (wean brace, light strengthening), Wks 8-16 (progressive strengthening), 16+ (RTS)
Notes: Thumb UCL repair (ulnar collateral ligament of the thumb/MCP joint); not elbow UCL; return to sport at 16 weeks

WB Status: FWB; long-arm splint 0-10 days; hinged brace 10 days-6 wks
Key Restrictions: Long-arm splint 0-10 days; hinged brace with progressive ROM 10 days-6 wks; no valgus stress 6 wks-3 months; no overhead triceps or heavy bench press; avoid hyperextension; competitive throwing not until 1 year
Timeline / Phases: 0-10 days (splint), 10 days-6 wks (hinged brace, AROM), 6 wks-3 months (no valgus stress), 3-4 months (arc toss 20-30 ft), 5-6 months (arc toss 60 ft), 7-8 months (straight line 25-50% velocity), 9-10 months (75% velocity), 11-12 months (competitive pitching at 1 year)
Notes: Tommy John surgery (elbow UCL); throwing progression begins at 3 months; competitive pitching at 1 year

WB Status: NWB 3-4 wks; FWB in boot by 6 wks; therapy begins 4 wks postop
Key Restrictions: No active or passive dorsiflexion past neutral until 8 wks; remove heel wedges starting 6 wks; discontinue boot 6-8 wks; discontinue night splint at 6 wks; discontinue heel wedge in shoe 12-16 wks; no gastroc/soleus stretching until 12 wks; single leg heel raise only when double leg heel raise is equal (12-16 wks)
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 4-6: NWB→FWB in boot), Phase II (Wks 6-12: boot to shoe with heel lift), Phase III (Wk 12-discharge: wean heel wedge, unilateral heel raise 12-16 wks, jogging 16-20 wks, agility 16-20 wks)
Notes: Jogging ~16-20 wks once single leg heel raise with good mechanics; agility drills 16-20 wks cleared by MD; monitor incision and tendon scar for mobility throughout

WB Status: NWB 3 wks; FWB in walking boot 3-6 wks; Aircast splint for day-to-day 6-12 wks
Key Restrictions: No passive inversion or forceful eversion 6 wks; avoid plantarflexion > resting position 4 wks; 90° immobilizer for 3 wks; immobilizer for sleeping 4 wks then Aircast 4-6 wks; no running, jumping, or ballistic activities 3 months; bike at 8 wks, elliptical at 10 wks; lace-up ankle support for athletics at 3-6 months
Timeline / Phases: 0-3 wks (NWB, posterior splint/90° immobilizer), 3-6 wks (WBAT in walking boot, Aircast day-to-day), 6-12 wks (increased intensity, full ROM cautious inversion/eversion), 3-6 months (RTS with lace-up ankle support)
Notes: For chronic ankle instability; MD appointments at day 8-10, 1 month, 2 months, 4 months, 6 months, and 1 year postop

WB Status: NWB 4 wks (splint); WBAT in walking boot from 4 wks; discontinue boot/crutches at 6 wks
Key Restrictions: Removable splint (foot dorsiflexion/neutral, great toe hyperextension) for 4 wks; NWB 4 wks; toe flexion limited to 0-10° at wks 3-4; no resistive exercises until 8-10 wks; turf toe protector in shoe from 6 wks onward
Timeline / Phases: Wks 3-4 (passive DF/toe ext, place and hold, 0-10° toe flex only), Wk 4 (active toe flex/PF with ankle neutral), Wk 5 (active DF/PF, passive toe flex), Wk 6 (DC boot/crutches, turf toe protector, full PROM), Wks 8+ (closed chain, resistive exercises)
Notes: EHL = Extensor Hallucis Longus (great toe extensor); therapy begins at 3 wks postop; heel insert in boot should be removed to maintain foot in dorsiflexion/neutral; no resistive exercises until 8-10 wks

WB Status: NWB until 4 wks; FWB in pneumatic boot by 6 wks; wean to shoe with ASO brace 6-12 wks
Key Restrictions: Short leg splint (ankle neutral) 10-14 days, then short leg cast to 4 wks; no active or passive ankle ROM phase I; avoid inversion/eversion until 6 wks; no barefoot walking; no plyometrics or change of direction until phase III; discontinue boot 6-8 wks; night splint DC at 6 wks; no return to high-impact until cleared by MD and PT; 90% calf strength required for discharge
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 0-4: NWB, splint/cast, no ankle ROM), Phase II (Wks 4-6: AROM, progress to FWB in boot), Phase III (Wks 6-12: wean to shoe/ASO brace, CKC, proprioception), Phase IV (Wks 16-24+: plyometrics, jogging 16-20 wks, RTS)
Notes: Also covers peroneal tendon debridement; jogging ~16-20 wks; agility 16-20 wks cleared by MD; 90% calf strength vs uninvolved for discharge

WB Status: NWB until 4 wks; FWB in boot by 6 wks; transition to supportive shoe with ASO brace by 8 wks
Key Restrictions: Short leg splint (ankle neutral) 10-14 days, then short leg cast to 4 wks; no active or passive ankle ROM phase I; avoid active inversion/eversion until cleared; no loaded plantarflexion initially; no barefoot walking; avoid high resistance or eccentric exercises until surgeon clearance; no running or jumping until later stages; discontinue boot 6-8 wks; night splint DC at 6 wks
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 0-4: NWB, splint/cast), Phase II (Wks 4-6: AROM, PWBFWB in boot), Phase III (Wks 6-12: shoe/ASO brace by wk 8, CKC, proprioception), Phase IV (Wks 12-24+: plyometrics, jogging 16-20 wks, RTS)
Notes: Jogging ~16-20 wks once single leg heel raise with good mechanics; agility 16-20 wks cleared by MD; 90% calf strength vs uninvolved for discharge; monitor posterior tibialis tendon for signs of tendinopathy

WB Status: NWB until 4 wks; FWB in boot by 6 wks; transition to supportive shoe at 8 wks
Key Restrictions: Short leg splint (ankle neutral) 10-14 days, then short leg cast to 4 wks; no active or passive ankle ROM phase I; avoid aggressive dorsiflexion and resisted plantarflexion phase II; no barefoot walking; no plyometrics until phase IV; discontinue boot at 8 wks; night splint DC at 6 wks; no return to high-impact until cleared by MD and PT; 90% calf strength required for discharge
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 0-4: NWB, splint/cast, no ankle ROM), Phase II (Wks 4-6: AROM PF/inv/ev, FWB in boot by wk 6), Phase III (Wks 6-12: shoe at 8 wks, CKC, proprioception), Phase IV (Wks 16-24+: plyometrics, jogging 16-20 wks, RTS)
Notes: Includes Achilles debridement, calcaneal bone spur excision, and tendon reattachment with suture anchors; jogging ~16-20 wks once single leg heel raise with good mechanics; 90% calf strength vs uninvolved for discharge

WB Status: NWB wks 3-4; PWBAT at 6 wks per MD; FWB progression thereafter
Key Restrictions: Removable splint keeps foot in dorsiflexion at all times; foot must NOT drop into plantarflexion when splint removed; no WB wks 3-4; foot movement limited to neutral or less unless specified; no resistive exercises until 8-10 wks; running and plyometrics only at 16-20 wks and only when cleared by MD
Timeline / Phases: Wks 3-4 (passive DF, place and hold, active PF 10-15°), Wk 4 (active DF begins, active PF 20-25°), Wk 5 (full AROM all directions), Wk 6 (PWBAT per MD, A/PROM all), Wk 8 (full AROM, resistive DF starts, CKC), Wks 12-16 (sport/recreation loading), Wks 16-20 (running/plyometrics per MD)
Notes: Protocol based on hand tendon repair early active motion principles; therapy begins 3 wks postop; tibialis anterior isolation at 8 wks; confirm with MD if repair is near aponeurosis or if tendon was shortened

WB Status: WBAT; stationary bike starts day 1 postop with minimal resistance
Key Restrictions: Stationary bike begins day 1 postop (minimal resistance); passive ROM emphasis on IR and circumduction wks 1-6; no running until wks 9-13; no lunges or plyometrics until wks 9-13; sports-specific training and functional testing not until wks 17-25
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 1-7: bike day 1, passive ROM/IR, prone lying, heel slides, bridges), Phase II (Wks 5-9: mini-squats, outdoor biking, swimming, lateral step-ups, elliptical), Phase III (Wks 9-13: lunges, water plyometrics, running, agility), Phase IV (Wks 17-25: sport-specific drills, sprinting, functional testing)
Notes: Covers labral repair, osteoplasty, and capsular repair; stationary bike day 1 postop is a key distinguishing feature; presented as an exercise-week chart format

WB Status: NWB in brace locked at 60° flexion 4 wks; TDWB wks 4-6; WBAT wks 6+
Key Restrictions: NWB brace locked at 60° flexion for 4 wks; avoid hip flexion coupled with knee extension for 6 wks; no resisted CKC hamstring exercise until 8 wks; no unilateral resistive hamstring activity until 12 wks; no running until 16 wks; full return to activity 4-6 months per MD clearance
Timeline / Phases: 0-4 wks (NWB, brace locked 60°), 4-6 wks (TDWB, wean crutches, transition out of brace), 6-8 wks (WBAT, CKC loading), 8-12 wks (gentle strengthening, no impact), 12-16 wks (dynamic/single-leg strength), 16-24 wks (RTS when cleared by MD)
Notes: Stationary cycling at 8 wks; outdoor cycling and swimming at 12 wks; running at 16 wks; sitting may benefit from donut cushion early postop; full return to activity 4-6 months

WB Status: FWBAT; walker wk 1; cane wk 2 (nonsurgical side); no device by wk 3
Key Restrictions: Minimal precautions due to anterolateral approach; limit toe-out and hip extension behind plane of body; cautious with external rotation; no forced hip ER; goal: independent ambulation without device by 3 wks; preoperative PT visit required
Timeline / Phases: 0-1 wks (walker, FWBAT, basic exercises), 1-2 wks (cane, stationary bike per MD, mini-squats, bridges), 2-4 wks (ambulate without device, step-up/down progression, lunges, single-leg balance)
Notes: Anterolateral approach has minimal hip precautions (compare to posterior approach); preop PT visit for HEP and precaution education; check with PT or MD before any activity unsure of

WB Status: FWBAT; walker wk 1; cane wk 2 (nonsurgical side); no device by wk 3
Key Restrictions: No resisted hip external rotation for 4-6 wks; NO standard hip precautions required — Direct Superior Technique has very low dislocation risk with normal activities; cautious with hip ER exercises wks 1-2; preoperative PT visit required; check with PT or MD before any unsure activity
Timeline / Phases: 0-1 wks (walker, FWBAT, basic HEP), 1-2 wks (cane, stationary bike per MD, mini-squats, bridges), 2-4 wks (ambulate without device, step-up/down progression, lunges, single-leg balance)
Notes: Direct Superior approach = NO standard hip precautions (contrast with posterior approach); only restriction is no resisted hip ER for 4-6 wks; preop PT visit for HEP and education

WB Status: TDWB first few weeks per physician; increase WB 25%/wk starting 4-8 wks; ambulate without device by 8-12 wks
Key Restrictions: TDWB first few weeks; hip precautions 1-12 wks: IR to 0° only, adduction to 0° only, flexion to 90° only; no hip IR during rotation exercises (ER to 30° only); stationary bike must not exceed 90° hip flexion; no adduction past midline
Timeline / Phases: 0-4 wks (TDWB, crutches/walker, basic exercises, hip precautions), 4-8 wks (increase WB 25%/wk per MD, cane, mini-squats, bridges), 8-12 wks (ambulate without device, aquatic program)
Notes: Strict hip precautions maintained 1-12 wks (IR 0°, adduction 0°, flex 90°); golf when off 2 crutches ~3-4 months; chip/putt ~3 months; driving ~4 months; golf cart recommended first 6 months

WB Status: N/A
Key Restrictions: Posterior splint at 90 deg wk 1; elbow ROM brace wks 2-6 (progressing 45-100 to 15-130 deg); no active elbow flexion or supination until wk 6; biceps isotonics not until wk 16
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (wk 1): posterior splint; Phase II (wks 2-6): ROM brace, shoulder/wrist/gripping exercises; Phase III (wks 6-10): discontinue brace wk 8, shoulder/wrist isotonics; Phase IV (wks 10-16): UBE; Phase V (wks 16-26): light biceps isotonics, plyometrics (two-handed wk 16, one-handed wk 20-22); Phase VI (wks 26+): sport-specific training and return to activity
Notes: SLU Sports Medicine and Shoulder Service; 6-phase protocol

WB Status: N/A
Key Restrictions: Elbow arthrofibrosis; progressive ROM as tolerated with no restrictions from week 1
Timeline / Phases: Acute (wks 1-6): elbow ROM emphasis, wrist/grip exercises, UBE, scapulothoracic assessment; Functional (wks 6-12): elbow/forearm strengthening, PNF patterns, endurance training; Maintenance (wks 12+): sport-specific training, phase out supervised rehab
Notes: SLU Sports Medicine and Shoulder Service; 4-month protocol, 2-3 visits/week; diagnosis: elbow arthrofibrosis

WB Status: N/A
Key Restrictions: Progressive ROM as tolerated with no restrictions from week 1
Timeline / Phases: Acute (wks 1-6): elbow ROM emphasis, wrist/grip exercises, UBE, scapulothoracic assessment; Functional (wks 6-12): elbow/forearm strengthening, PNF patterns, endurance training; Maintenance (wks 12+): sport-specific training, phase out supervised rehab
Notes: SLU Sports Medicine and Shoulder Service; 4-month protocol, 2-3 visits/week; diagnosis: elbow arthritis / loose body

WB Status: N/A
Key Restrictions: Lateral epicondylitis; avoid overloading wrist extensors during acute phase (wks 1-6)
Timeline / Phases: Acute (wks 1-6): elbow ROM, avoid wrist extensor overload, scapulothoracic assessment, UBE; Functional (wks 6-12): elbow/forearm strengthening, PNF patterns, endurance training; Maintenance (wks 12+): sport-specific training, phase out supervised rehab
Notes: SLU Sports Medicine and Shoulder Service; 4-month protocol, 2-3 visits/week; diagnosis: lateral epicondylitis

WB Status: N/A
Key Restrictions: Splint at 70 deg wk 0-1; brace 40-80 deg wks 1-4; avoid valgus stress at elbow; no ER strengthening until wk 6-8; interval throwing program not until wk 14-24
Timeline / Phases: Phase 1 (0-1 wk): splint at 70 deg, gripping/wrist ROM; Phase 2 (1-4 wks): brace 40-80 deg, pain-free isometrics; Phase 3 (4-6 wks): brace 20-90 deg, light isotonics, avoid valgus; Phase 4 (6-8 wks): DC brace, ER strengthening begins; Phase 5 (8-10 wks): eccentrics, PNF, aggressive shoulder; Phase 6 (10-13 wks): plyometrics; Phase 7 (14-24 wks): interval throwing program
Notes: SLU Sports Medicine and Shoulder Service; 7-phase protocol; 4-month duration, 2-3 visits/week; 2022 version

WB Status: N/A
Key Restrictions: Brace fixed at 90 deg wk 0-1; brace 30-110 deg wk 2; 10-125 deg wk 3; unrestricted ROM wk 4; avoid valgus stress; no IR wk 1; expedited return to throwing vs. reconstruction
Timeline / Phases: Phase 1 (wk 0-1): brace at 90 deg, wrist ROM, pain-free isometrics; Phase 2 (wks 2-5): progressive brace opening, PROM/AAROM, UBE, scapular training; (see full document for remaining phases)
Notes: SLU/SSM Health PT Orthopedic Residency; evidence-based protocol; internal brace allows expedited rehab vs. reconstruction

WB Status: 20 lbs PWB for 6 weeks; progress to FWB by week 8; wean crutches weeks 8-10
Key Restrictions: No active abduction, IR, or passive ER/adduction for 6 weeks; CPM 4 hrs/day; 20 lb WB restriction through week 6
Timeline / Phases: Wks 0-4: CPM, bike, PROM (hip flexion to 90 deg), gait training PWB; Wks 4-6: progress hip flexion >90 deg, bridges, adduction isotonics; Wks 6-8: progress to 100% WB by wk 8, ROM progression; Wks 8-10: wean crutches, progressive LE strengthening, proprioception, elliptical; Wks 10-12: progressive LE/core, balance, treadmill side-stepping; Wks 12+: treadmill running, sport-specific agility/plyometrics
Notes: SLU Sports Medicine and Shoulder Service; arthroscopic gluteus medius + labral tear; adapted from bryankellymd.com; 4-month protocol, 2-3 visits/week

Surgery Type: Femoroacetabuloplasty with or without Labral Repair
WB Status: WBAT with crutches; wean off crutches week 3; FWB by ~week 6
Key Restrictions: Avoid combined hip motions/excessive flexion; avoid early iliopsoas activation; no isotonic hip flexion until week 8; WBAT (not toe-touch) with crutches
Timeline / Phases: Wks 0-6: wean crutches week 3, isometrics (no hip flexion wks 1-2), isotonics (no hip flexion wks 3-4), bike; Wks 4-10: CKC activities (squat, step up/down), single-limb balance, no isotonic hip flexion until wk 8; Wks 6-12: progressive CKC/single-leg strengthening, elliptical, advanced balance; Wks 12+: plyometrics, running progression (track/treadmill program), sport-specific drills
Notes: SLU/SSM Health PT Orthopedic Residency; evidence-based with references; discharge uses mHHS, HOS-SSS, iHOT-12 PROs; updated 7/8/21; covers FAI with or without labral repair

WB Status: WBAT week 1; gait without crutches by week 2; brace and graft type per surgeon
Key Restrictions: If meniscal repair: brace locked at 0 deg until week 5; no loaded knee flexion >45 deg until week 5; no loaded >90 deg until week 8; graft protection per surgeon
Timeline / Phases: Week 1: AROM/PROM 0-90 deg, quad sets, SLR; Week 2: flexion >110 deg, gait without crutches; Wks 3-5: flexion within 10 deg of contralateral, quad 4+/5; Wks 6-8: full ROM, begin running progression; Wks 9-12: hop tests >85%, agility; Wks 13-RTS: all hop tests >90%, Modified SEBT symmetry; 4-6-12 month follow-up functional testing
Notes: SLU/SSM Health PT Orthopedic Residency; milestone-based evidence-based protocol; 2022 version; PTB and hamstring graft; concomitant injuries dictate progression; uses Soreness Rules and Stroke Test for effusion grading

WB Status: NWB in brace 2 weeks; 25-50% PWB in brace locked wks 2-6; WBAT wks 6-14; running at 4 months if criteria met
Key Restrictions: NWB in brace wks 1-2; brace locked in extension; no running/sport until week 14+; running criteria: extensor LSI >70%, AROM 0->125 deg, hop test >70%; no descending stairs reciprocally until adequate quad control
Timeline / Phases: Wks 1-6: NWB then 25-50% PWB brace locked, SLR/quad sets, patellar mob; Wks 6-14: WBAT, normalize gait, bike (short crank then standard at >115 deg), leg press, step program, elliptical, retro treadmill; Wks 14-22: running, lunges, agility, plyometrics; discharge: hop test >85% and isokinetic test >85% limb symmetry
Notes: SLU Sports Medicine; covers both HTO and DFO; 4-month protocol 2-3 visits/week

WB Status: NWB wks 1-2; TTWB wks 3-9; WBAT week 9; DC crutches when stable
Key Restrictions: Brace locked in extension wks 1-9; TTWB (no WB with knee flexed) through week 8; no active knee flexion or hamstrings wks 1-6; hamstring flexion OK week 7; no knee flexion WB through week 9
Timeline / Phases: Wks 1-2: NWB TTWB, brace locked, PROM 0-40 deg; Wks 3-6: TTWB, ROM 0-90 deg; Wk 7: advance ROM, bike, hamstring activation; Wk 8: AAROM, leg press 0-70 deg; Wk 9: WBAT; Wk 10: mini-squats, step ups, leg press 0-90 deg; Wk 11: DC brace, retro treadmill, 8-in step ups; Wk 12: running if LSI>70%/hop>70%; Wks 12-22: sport-specific progression; 6-12 months: return to sport (quad >90%, hop >90%)
Notes: SLU Sports Medicine; diagnosis: ACL, PCL, LCL tears; 4-month protocol 2-3 visits/week

WB Status: NWB wks 1-2; TTWB wks 3-9; WBAT week 9; DC crutches when stable

Surgery Type: Hip Arthroscopy for FAI with Labral Repair — Pediatric
WB Status: PWB 20 lbs foot-flat step-to with bilateral crutches wks 0-3; WBAT wks 4-6; wean crutches by 6-8 wks

Notes: Revised 3/2023; 5-phase protocol; labral repair vs reconstruction: reconstruction adds 4+ weeks to each phase; circumduction critical for adhesion prevention (early post-op); outcome measures: HOS or LEFS; pediatric-specific considerations: open apophyses, cam deformity prevalence in adolescent athletes, femoral neck fracture risk with head-neck resection

WB Status: PWB 25% with brace locked in extension wks 0-2; WBAT Phase II; FWB Phase III
Key Restrictions: Brace locked, PWB 25% wks 0-2; unlock brace once SLR without lag; DC brace at 6 wks per surgeon; knee flexion limited: 30 deg wk 1/60 deg wk 2/90 deg wk 4/110 deg wk 6; concomitant tibial tuberosity osteotomy (TTO) alters WB/ROM/assistive device — confirm with surgeon; adductor strengthening emphasized throughout; no plyometrics until Phase IV
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (0-2 wks): PWB 25% brace locked, patellar PROM, NMES 2500 Hz, quad sets, SLR (no lag), hip abduction, standing hamstring curl; Phase II (3-6 wks): WBAT, stationary bike, adductor strengthening (ball squeezes, SLR adduction, bridging with ball squeeze), squats 0-60 deg, balance; Phase III (7-12 wks): FWB, elliptical/stair climber ~8 wks, single leg progression (step-ups, step-downs, SL squats, lateral lunges, RDL), perturbation training; Phase IV (13-16 wks): bilateral plyometrics, progress balance; Phase V (4-5 mo): return-to-run, agility/plyometrics; Phase VI (6+ mo): multi-plane cutting/pivoting, non-contact to full play ~6-7 mo
Notes: Revised 3/2024; 6-phase protocol; discharge Phase V: quad/HS/glut >=95%, H:Q >=66%, hop >=95%, Lysholm >90%, KOOS-sports >90%, IKDC >93, ACL-RSI; Phase VI: no additional criteria; NMES 2500 Hz 75 bursts; TTO concomitant procedure requires surgeon consultation on restrictions

WB Status: TDWB with brace locked wks 0-4; PWB 25% wks 4-6; FWB wks 6-8
Key Restrictions: Brace always locked in extension wks 0-6; no knee flexion wks 0-2; flexion to 60 deg wks 2-4; flexion to 90 deg wks 4-6; surgeon clearance required to advance WB at 6-wk follow-up; no impact loading until Phase VI (6+ mo); patellar mobs in Phase I for condylar lesion only (patellar lesion requires surgeon clearance); Pedi-IKDC preferred over KOOS-child
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (0-2 wks): TDWB brace locked, patellar mobs (condylar lesion), low-load extension stretches, quad sets, hip ABD/ADD/extension, SLR; Phase II (2-4 wks): TDWB brace locked, flexion to 60 deg, heel slides, 4-way SLR, AAROM short arc quad; Phase III (4-6 wks): PWB 25% brace locked, flexion to 90 deg, stationary bike semi-revolutions, AROM knee flex/ext with light resistance; Phase IV (6-8 wks): FWB, wean brace/crutches, walking program 15-30 min, stationary bike moderate resistance, hamstring curls, leg press; Phase V (9-12 wks): progressive strengthening, walking to 45 min, bilateral plyometrics (LSI >75%); Phase VI (3-6 mo): return-to-run program, agility/plyometrics, full sport at 7 mo
Notes: Revised 03/2024; 6-phase protocol; Phase VI criteria: quad/HS/glut >=90%, H:Q >=66%, hop >=90%; no impact loading throughout Phases I-V; strict brace/WB protocol with surgeon clearance at 6 wks; machine sizing guidance for adolescents; Pedi-IKDC recommended; hard cutting/pivoting ~7 mo

WB Status: Sling first 2 weeks; avoid PROM >90 deg elevation Phase I; progressive UE use Phase II+
Key Restrictions: No PROM >90 deg elevation Phase I; sling x 2 wks; progressive RC strengthening; closed chain deferred to Phase IV
Timeline / Phases: 4 phases: Phase I (0-6 wks, sling, PROM <90 deg elevation), Phase II (7-12 wks, progressive PROM/AAROM), Phase III (13-18 wks, RC strengthening, overhead), Phase IV (19+ wks, advanced strengthening, return to sport)
Notes: Revised October 2021; AC joint reconstruction; avoid PROM >90 deg elevation Phase I; sling 2 wks; progressive RC and periscapular strengthening; closed chain Phase IV

WB Status: Full immobilization Phase I (0-8 wks); no formal PT Phase I; progressive UE use Phase II+
Key Restrictions: No formal PT Phase I (0-8 wks); AAROM only Phase II (8-16 wks); NO ER beyond 0 degrees Phase II; no PROM (do not stretch arm) Phase II-III
Timeline / Phases: 4 phases: Phase I (0-8 wks, full immobilization, no formal PT), Phase II (8-16 wks, AAROM only — NO ER beyond neutral), Phase III (16-24 wks, gentle band strengthening — scaption/ER priority), Phase IV (24+ wks, progressive strengthening, return to sport)
Notes: For irreparable subscapularis tear; full immobilization Phase I — no formal PT is critical distinction; NO ER beyond neutral Phase II; scaption and ER retraining priority Phase III+; swimming not until Phase IV

WB Status: Sling per surgeon; no AROM shoulder or elbow Phase I (wks 1-4)
Key Restrictions: ER limited to 40 deg Phase I; no AROM elbow or shoulder Phase I (wks 1-4); no biceps/resisted elbow flexion loading until Phase III (wks 6-8)
