Timeline / Phases: 5 phases: Phase I (wks 1-4, sling, PROM ER ≤40, no AROM), Phase II (4-6 wks, AAROM begins), Phase III (6-8 wks, AROM begins, biceps loading begins), Phase IV (8-12 wks, progressive strengthening), Phase V (12-16 wks, return to sport)
Notes: Revised October 2021; no isolated biceps loading until Phase III (wks 6-8); ER limit 40 deg Phase I; sling per surgeon; 5-phase protocol

WB Status: Sling; no PROM ER past neutral until wk 5; no AROM until wk 5
Key Restrictions: No PROM ER past neutral until wk 5; no AROM until wk 5; subscapularis protection; significant long-term ER limitation; bony procedure — bone healing drives progression
Timeline / Phases: 5 phases: Phase I (0-6 wks, sling, no ER past neutral, no AROM), Phase II (7-8 wks, PROM/AAROM begins), Phase III (9-12 wks, AROM begins, gentle strengthening), Phase IV (13-20 wks, progressive strengthening), Phase V (21-28 wks, return to sport)
Notes: Revised 12/2021; bony procedure with coracoid graft and subscapularis considerations; no ER past neutral until wk 5; 28-week protocol; subscapularis protection is key distinction from soft-tissue stabilization

WB Status: Gunslinger sling Phase I (0-8 wks); full immobilization
Key Restrictions: Full immobilization Phase I (0-8 wks); AAROM only Phase II (8-16 wks); NO IR Phase II (critical); no PROM/aggressive stretching Phase II-III; no swimming until Phase IV
Timeline / Phases: 4 phases: Phase I (0-8 wks, gunslinger sling, full immobilization), Phase II (8-16 wks, AAROM only — NO IR), Phase III (16-24 wks, gentle strengthening — scaption/ER priority), Phase IV (24+ wks, advanced strengthening, return to sport)
Notes: For massive irreparable posterior/superior RC tear; gunslinger sling Phase I; NO IR Phase II is critical precaution; prioritize scaption and ER retraining for lower trapezius in new role; swimming not until Phase IV

WB Status: Sling neutral or IR Phase I-II; WB through surgical side permitted Phase III (wks 6-8)
Key Restrictions: No IR isometrics Phase I-II; no pec major loading until Phase IV (9-14 wks); ER limited early (neutral progressing 5 deg/wk); repair-type dependent progression (bone-tendon vs tendon-tendon vs muscle-tendon); sling wean timing differs by repair type
Timeline / Phases: 7 phases: Phase I (0-3 wks, sling, PROM ER neutral progressing 5 deg/wk, flex 45 deg, ABD 30 deg), Phase II (4-6 wks, AAROM, sling wean per repair type), Phase III (6-8 wks, AROM, WB permitted), Phase IV (9-14 wks, IR/pec isometrics begin, full ROM goal bone-tendon), Phase V (14-20 wks, pec isotonics, full ROM goal tendon/muscle-tendon), Phase VI (5-6 months, plyometrics, early RTS), Phase VII (6+ months, unrestricted RTS — bench press 50% 1RM)
Notes: Revised 7/2023; repair type (bone-tendon, tendon-tendon, muscle-tendon) drives sling wean and ROM milestones; no pec loading until Phase IV; axillary incision monitoring for infection risk; 7-phase protocol

WB Status: Sling if recommended; avoid heavy lifting and overhead activities Phase I
Key Restrictions: Avoid heavy lifting and overhead activities; focus on anterior deltoid re-education and compensation; pain-guided progression throughout; outcomes vary compared to surgical intervention
Timeline / Phases: 4 phases: Phase I (0-4 wks, protection/pain management — PROM/AAROM, ice, NSAIDs, injections as indicated), Phase II (4-8 wks, restoring ROM — anterior deltoid isometrics progressing to isotonics, scapular stabilization), Phase III (8-12 wks, progressive strengthening — remaining RC muscles, closed chain), Phase IV (12+ wks, return to function — activity-specific exercises)
Notes: Revised 8/2023; for massive RC tears not appropriate for surgery; anterior deltoid re-education is central strategy to compensate for absent cuff; multidisciplinary team recommended; outcomes inferior to surgical management

WB Status: Sling with abduction pillow (30-45 deg ABD) x 6 wks; neutral rotation
Key Restrictions: No AROM/AAROM Phase I; no biceps contraction (elbow AROM) x 6 wks; ER <30 deg scapular plane Phase I; ER <45 deg Phase II; AROM begins wks 7-8; resisted elbow flexion not until wk 12
Timeline / Phases: 6 phases: Phase I (0-3 wks, sling + ABD pillow, PROM ER<30/FF<90, no AROM/AAROM/biceps), Phase II (4-6 wks, PROM ER<45/FF<120, AAROM begins, no AROM or biceps), Phase III (7-8 wks, AROM + elbow flexion begins, sling D/C), Phase IV (9-12 wks, full PROM/AROM, resisted elbow flexion at wk 12), Phase V (13-16 wks, advanced strengthening, RTS criteria), Phase VI (4-6 months, return to sport)
Notes: Revised 10/2021; abduction pillow 30-45 deg x 6 wks distinguishes from Bankart protocols; no biceps/elbow AROM x 6 wks; RTS criteria: ER/IR ≥85% uninvolved and ER/IR ratio ≥60%; negative impingement/instability signs required

WB Status: Sling x 6 wks (or per surgeon); no GH PROM; no scapular AROM Phase I (0-6 wks)
Key Restrictions: No GH PROM or AROM Phase I (0-6 wks); no scapular AROM (protraction, retraction, depression, elevation) Phase I; no bearing weight through involved extremity; GH PROM/AAROM begins 6-8 wks; scapular AROM and strengthening at 8 wks; no lifting >5 lbs until 9 wks; no lifting restrictions at ~4 months
Timeline / Phases: 3 phases: Phase I (0-6 wks, sling, elbow/wrist/hand AROM only — NO shoulder or scapular motion), Phase II (6-12 wks, GH PROM/AAROM at 6-8 wks, scapular AROM + strengthening at 8 wks, wean sling), Phase III (13+ wks, progressive strengthening to 90 deg ABD, sport-specific; no lifting restrictions at ~4 months)
Notes: No shoulder or scapular motion Phase I (most restrictive initial phase); tendon autograft harvest site possible; GH and scapular rehab both delayed until wks 6-8; RTS requires 5/5 RC strength at 90 deg ABD; QuickDASH and PENN outcome measures used

WB Status: NWB crutches wks 0-2; WBAT in boot wks 3-6; WBAT in shoe with active ankle brace wks 7-8+
Key Restrictions: No inversion or PF ROM wks 0-2; avoid inversion throughout early phases; ankle mobs must avoid stress to CFL and ATFL; more conservative for tendon augmentation, peroneal repair, revision, hyperlaxity, or comorbidities
Timeline / Phases: 6 phases: Phase I (0-2 wks, NWB, hip/quad strengthening), Phase II (3-6 wks, WBAT boot — DF/PF/eversion ROM, no inversion; submax isometrics except inversion), Phase III (7-8 wks, shoe + active brace — full ROM, calf raises, SL balance, pool jogging), Phase IV (9-12 wks, SL calf raises, squats, Y-balance), Phase V (3-4 months, lateral lunges, SL progression, elliptical), Phase VI (5-6+ months, interval running, agility/plyometric program)
Notes: Revised 12/2021; RTS criteria: 25 SL heel raises, 90% Y-balance, 90% single-leg hop and triple hop, CAIT or FAAM; Phase VI unrestricted RTS at 5-6+ months; avoid stress to CFL and ATFL with mobs throughout

WB Status: NWB in splint/cast wks 0-2; partial progressive WB in boot wks 2-4; FWB boot (no SPR repair) wks 4-6 or (SPR repaired) wk 6+; wean boot wk 6 (no SPR) or wk 8 (SPR repaired)
Key Restrictions: If SPR repaired: no inversion/eversion until wk 6; if SPR repaired: no isometric eversion until wk 6; SPR repair status is the critical variable driving all phase progression timelines; no running until 3-6 months
Timeline / Phases: 6 phases: Phase I (0-2 wks, NWB splint, hip/quad strengthening, elevation toes above nose), Phase II (2-4 wks, partial WB boot — PROM/AROM DF/PF/inversion/eversion if no SPR repair; DF/PF only if SPR repaired until wk 6), Phase III (4-8 wks, FWB boot — eversion resistance begins; wean boot per SPR status), Phase IV (9-12 wks, shoe — bilateral heel raises, squats, SL balance, Alter-G), Phase VI (3-6 months, SL heel raises, eccentric calf, lunges, return-to-run program, beginner plyometrics), Phase VII (6+ months, agility program, sport-specific; LE functional tests ≥90%)
Notes: Revised 10/2021; SPR repair status is key clinical variable — dictates inversion/eversion and eversion strengthening timeline; tendoscopic vs open approach may also affect progression; elevation toes above nose Phase I; PRRS for RTS; LE functional tests ≥90% contralateral required; Phase V missing from original document (jumps to Phase VI)

WB Status: NWB on repaired UE until wk 8; WB progression wk 8 (elbow flexed) → wk 10 (elbow extended); no lifting until wk 8
Key Restrictions: AVOID active elbow flexion and forearm supination until wk 4; no lifting with repaired UE until wk 8; NWB until wk 8; hinged brace extension progressively released weekly (wk 2: 90-full, wk 3: 45-full, wk 4: 30-full, wk 5: 20-full, wk 6: D/C brace)
Timeline / Phases: 6 phases: Phase I (0-1 wk, posterior splint 90 deg, grip strengthening only), Phase II (2-6 wks, hinged brace extension serially released; PROM wk 2; AROM begins wk 4), Phase III (7-10 wks, WB begins wk 8, composite ROM, scapulothoracic strength), Phase IV (11-15 wks, submaximal isometrics at wk 10 → isotonic biceps/supination wks 10-12; shoulder strengthening), Phase V (4-6 months, bilateral UE plyometrics after wk 16; single-arm after wks 20-22), Phase VI (6+ months, sport-specific; interval throwing program)
Notes: Revised 10/2021; serial hinged brace extension release weekly; no active elbow flexion/supination until wk 4; WB progresses from elbow flexed (wk 8) to extended (wk 10); throwing discharge criteria: ER/IR ratio 72-76%, KJOC throwers score; physician clearance required

WB Status: N/A — non-operative; activity modification throughout
Key Restrictions: Minimize aggravating activities; wrist stretches at 90 deg elbow flexion Phase II (progressing to full extension Phase III); eccentric loading emphasized Phase III; joint protection and ergonomics education Phase II+
Timeline / Phases: 3 phases: Phase I (0-2 wks, acute — STM/IASTM, dry needling, joint mob/manipulation, BFR, splinting/taping, nerve mob, stretching, modalities), Phase II (2-4 wks, sub-acute — wrist F/E isometrics, scapular stabilization, wrist stretches at 90 deg elbow flex), Phase III (4-6+ wks, late/chronic — eccentric/concentric wrist F/E and forearm P/S, MWM, wrist stretches at full elbow extension, plyometrics program)
Notes: Revised April 2021; covers BOTH medial (flexor-pronator) and lateral (ECRB) epicondylalgia; eccentric loading Phase III; MWM (mobilization with movement); RTS criteria: 90% strength vs contralateral; PRTEE and DASH outcome measures; differential diagnosis includes radial tunnel syndrome, ulnar nerve entrapment, C6 cervical pathology

Surgery Type: UCL Reconstruction (Palmaris Longus or Gracilis Graft)
WB Status: No WB restrictions — UE protocol; hinged brace wks 1-6; posterior splint at 90 deg wk 1
Key Restrictions: Avoid valgus stress to elbow throughout; no shoulder ER isometrics Phase I; no ER strengthening until wk 6; no throwing until Phase V (wk 16+); gracilis graft: no hamstring strengthening until wk 6; D/C brace at wk 6 if full ROM achieved
Timeline / Phases: 5 phases: Phase I (0-3 wks, posterior splint wk 1 at 90 deg; hinged brace wks 2-3 at 25-115 deg; isometrics shoulder/elbow; wrist AROM; elbow AROM begins day 14), Phase II (4-6 wks, brace extension progressed to full; wrist/forearm strengthening at 1 lb; biceps; scapular stabilization), Phase III (6-12 wks, no throwing; Thrower's 10 at wk 6; Advanced Thrower's 10 at wk 8; eccentric elbow F/E; 85% strength criteria), Phase IV (12-16 wks, 2-handed plyometrics at wk 12; 1-arm plyometrics; hitting program at wk 12), Phase V (16+ wks, interval throwing program; return to full sport)
Notes: Revised 6/2021; Tommy John surgery protocol; no throwing until 16+ wks; Thrower's 10 begins wk 6; throwing RTS criteria: ER/IR ratio 72-76%, ER/ABD ratio 68-73%, KJOC throwers score, physician clearance; gracilis graft adds knee ROM immediately post-op and bike at wk 3

WB Status: WBAT; activity modification to avoid intra-abdominal pressure increases
Key Restrictions: Avoid lifting or activities increasing intra-abdominal pressure; criterion-based (NOT time-based — healing times vary greatly); covers adductor-related, inguinal-related, pubic-related, and iliopsoas-related groin pain
Timeline / Phases: 4 criterion-based phases: Phase I Acute (pain control, adductor isometrics, TrA/quadruped/bridge progressions, walking/elliptical/bike), Phase II Subacute (PRE concentric/eccentric hip, Copenhagen adduction progressions, return-to-run protocol), Phase III Early RTS (plyometrics, agility, sprinting programs), Phase IV Unrestricted RTS (non-contact → full practice → full play)
Notes: Revised 1/2023; criterion-based not time-based; covers groin pain per DOHA 2015 classification (adductor-, inguinal-, pubic-, iliopsoas-related); RTS: hip index ≥90% HHD/isokinetic, adductor/abductor ratio >80%, hop testing ≥90%, HAGOS questionnaire >90%; 33 diagnostic terms in literature — DOHA umbrella term used

WB Status: WBAT; walking 15 min 2x/day Phase I; avoid activities increasing intra-abdominal pressure (Valsalva) Phases I-III
Key Restrictions: Avoid lifting and activities increasing intra-abdominal pressure (Valsalva) Phases I-III; no eccentric adductor loading until Phase IV (wk 6-8); hip index <20% required before return to jogging
Timeline / Phases: 6 phases: Phase I (0-1 wk, incision mob, quad/glute sets, ankle pumps, walking 15 min 2x/day), Phase II (2-3 wks, adductor isometrics, TrA/quadruped/bridge progressions, SLR, walking 30 min), Phase III (4-5 wks, PRE concentric hip, plank progressions, 4-way hip strengthening, elliptical), Phase IV (6-8 wks, Copenhagen adduction progressions, return-to-jog program, hip index <20%), Phase V (9-12 wks, plyometrics/agility/sprinting — hip index ≥90%; hop testing ≥90%; HAGOS >90%), Phase VI (3 months, non-contact → full practice → full play)
Notes: Revised 12/2021; surgical sports hernia/core muscle repair; progression mirrors non-operative athletic pubalgia protocol but time-based; RTS criteria: hip index ≥90%, adductor/abductor ratio >80%, hop testing ≥90%, HAGOS >90%; unrestricted RTS at 3 months

WB Status: WBAT throughout unless otherwise directed; no crutch requirement stated
Key Restrictions: Do NOT stretch hamstring Phase I; nerve gliding (sciatic neural flossing) if neural tension; no passive hamstring stretching until Phase II; avoid positions lengthening hamstring during upper body/core training Phase I; jog/walk may begin at wk 6 if criteria met
Timeline / Phases: 5 phases: Phase I (0-2 wks, quad/glute sets, UBE, hip/knee AAROM, no hamstring stretching), Phase II (2-4 wks, gentle pain-free HS stretch supine, low DL bridge, bike), Phase III (4-8 wks, bridge progressions, HS curls antigravity, step ups at wk 6; jog/walk at wk 6 if criteria met), Phase IV (8-12 wks, eccentric HS — ball rollout, deadlifts, bridge on ball; continuous then interval jogging), Phase V (12+ wks, Nordic HS, kettle bell, sprint progressions, plyometrics, agility ladder, 40 yd sprints at 90%)
Notes: Revised 12/2021; DO NOT stretch hamstring Phase I (sciatic flossing only if neural tension); LSI criteria to run: HS >80-90%, glute med >90%, quad >90%, hop cluster >85%; RTS: LSI HS >95%, glute/quad >95%, hop cluster >95%; PRRS psychological readiness scale used; covers proximal, mid-belly, and distal hamstring variants

WB Status: TDWB Phase I (0-2 wks); PWB 50% Phase II (2-6 wks); wean to FWB Phase III (6-12 wks)
Key Restrictions: Post-op hip brace limiting hip flexion to 45° at all times Phase I; hip flexion ROM limit 60° Phase I-II; brace hip flexion limit progresses to 90° by wk 6; no active hamstrings or hip extension until Phase III; D/C brace at 6-8 wks per MD; no running until Phase V (wks 16-20); no sprinting/speed work until Phase VI (wks 20-24)
Timeline / Phases: 6 phases: Phase I (0-2 wks, TDWB, brace at 45 deg hip flex limit, ankle pumps/quad sets/UBE only), Phase II (2-6 wks, PWB 50%, brace progresses to 90 deg by wk 6, low DL bridge, calf raises — NO active HS or hip extension), Phase III (6-12 wks, wean to FWB, D/C brace 6-8 wks, HS curls/hip extension antigravity, step ups/downs at wk 10), Phase IV (13-16 wks, eccentric HS — ball rollout, DL/SL deadlifts, walk-to-jog prep), Phase V (16-20 wks, walk-to-jog progression, no sprinting), Phase VI (20-24 wks, sprint progressions, plyometrics, jump training at wk 22)
Notes: Revised 10/2021; hip flexion brace is critical Phase I-II; no active hamstring strengthening until Phase III (wk 6+); progression parallels non-op hamstring but significantly delayed; RTS: LSI HS >95%, glute/quad >95%, hop cluster >95%; brace D/C and crutch wean per MD; covers chronicity, retraction length, and tendon count as outcome variables

WB Status: N/A — relative rest x 3 days; then sub-symptom threshold activity; no contact until Phase V
Key Restrictions: No complete rest beyond 72 hours (current literature); activities at sub-symptom threshold throughout; limit head movement and avoid Valsalva Phase II; no contact until Phase V; refer to concussion specialty clinic if not progressing as expected
Timeline / Phases: 5 phases: Phase I (0-3 days, relative rest — household/community activities as tolerated), Phase II (Intermediate — light aerobic: biking/walking; limit head movement; goal 85% age-adjusted HR without symptoms), Phase III (Late — running/skating/swimming/footwork; increased head movement; cognitive demands: math, talking, plays while exercising), Phase IV (Transitional — sport-specific drills, passing/shooting, no contact), Phase V (Full RTS — scrimmage first, then game play if asymptomatic)
Notes: Revised 9/2021; active rehab replaces complete rest after 72 hours per current literature; sub-symptom threshold is the governing principle; formal exertional testing recommended — refer to specialist if unfamiliar; multidisciplinary team approach emphasized; concussion specialty clinic referral if not progressing

WB Status: WBAT; cessation of athletic activity may be recommended Phase I-II
Key Restrictions: ICF classification: LBP with mobility deficits (restricted spinal ROM and segmental mobility); Oswestry Questionnaire for functional outcomes; cessation of athletic activity may be recommended Phases I-II
Timeline / Phases: 4 phases: Phase I (0-4 wks, 4-6 visits — pain control, STM, lumbar/thoracic/hip joint mob/manipulation, local TA/MF core, hip strengthening, walking/bike), Phase II (4-8 wks, 4-6 visits — scapular strengthening, neutral trunk stabilization: plank/dead bug/bird dog, hip and closed-chain strengthening), Phase III (8-12 wks, 4 visits — anti-rotation, squats, deadlifts, overhead press, return to run, plyometrics), Phase IV (12+ wks — sport-specific load progression, medicine ball, reactive training)
Notes: April 2025; ICF-classified LBP with mobility deficits — not radicular; lumbar/thoracic/hip manipulation in Phase I; Oswestry Questionnaire; discharge criteria: pre-injury performance level without pain; front plank 40 sec and side bridge 40 sec required for Phase III

WB Status: WBAT; avoid prolonged sitting and flexion-biased positions; light bracing may help Phase I
Key Restrictions: Limit sitting; avoid activities/positions that worsen symptoms (flexion>extension); controlled shift/extension to neutral; consider ESI referral if unable to sit for short periods with continued lower leg symptoms after 10-day NSAID course; monitor neurologic signs throughout; bracing discontinued Phase III
Timeline / Phases: 4 phases: Phase I (0-6 wks, 6-8 visits — ice/e-stim, self-traction, controlled extension to neutral, STM paraspinals, manual/mechanical traction, local TA/MF core — TA with heel slides/arm movements, walking short distances frequently), Phase II (6-12 wks, 8-10 visits — hip/thoracic mob, dead bug, bird dog, standing partial squats; ESI referral if needed; sit 20 min with neutral spine by end), Phase III (12-16 wks, 4-6 visits — anti-rotation, deadlifts, overhead press, plyometrics, return to run; bracing discontinued), Phase IV (16+ wks, optional — sport-specific with lumbopelvic control)
Notes: March 2025; for acute to subacute single-level disc herniation in 25-55 year old population; centralization concept used — ideally no symptoms below knee by end Phase I; reassuring language about body's ability to heal emphasized; ESI consult recommended if not improving by Phase II; front plank 40 sec and side bridge 40 sec required for Phase III

WB Status: WBAT; lumbar spine unloading in 90-90 position; assistive device (cane, poles, rollator) as needed for spinal unloading
Key Restrictions: Avoid prolonged sitting, bending, lifting, and forced lumbar extension (flexion-biased approach); spinal unloading in 90-90 position; assistive device recommended for spinal unloading; aquatics with modified stroke to reduce extension; avoid lumbar extension with strength training Phase III
Timeline / Phases: 4 phases: Phase I (0-6 wks, 6-8 visits — lumbar unloading 90-90, frequent position changes, STM, traction in hook-lying/90-90, aquatics with modified stroke, NuStep/recumbent bike/treadmill with incline, local TA/MF core, sit-to-stand), Phase II (6-12 wks, 6 visits — clamshell, squat/lunge with support, step-up, balance progression, reduce/D/C lumbar support), Phase III (12-16 wks, 2-4 visits — squat progression, plantigrade plank, avoid extension with strength training, return to gym), Phase IV (16+ wks, optional — sport/recreational specific with modified strokes for extension-sensitive activities)
Notes: March 2025; flexion-biased approach throughout (unlike disc herniation); neurogenic claudication management with 90-90 unloading and assistive devices; FABQ and STarT Back Tool for chronic cases; Oswestry Questionnaire; discharge: no pain increase with sport-specific exercise and proper mechanics

WB Status: WBAT; bracing may be recommended Phase I; avoid lumbar extension with all activity Phase I
Key Restrictions: AVOID lumbar extension with activity, positioning, manual therapy, and exercise Phase I (critical — pars interarticularis stress fracture); bracing Phase I; no lumbar extension criteria to progress Phase I; avoid lumbar extension with strength training throughout
Timeline / Phases: 4 phases: Phase I (0-6 wks, 2-4 visits — no lumbar extension, STM, thoracic/hip mob, local TA/MF core, hip strengthening, walking/bike/NuStep), Phase II (6-9 wks, 4-6 visits — neutral stabilization: dead bug/bird dog/side-bridge, hip strengthening, closed chain — squats/hip ER/side-step, D/C brace if pain-free ADLs), Phase III (9-12 wks, 4-6 visits — front/side plank, anti-rotation, squats, deadlifts, overhead press, plyometrics, return to run), Phase IV (12+ wks — sport-specific, medicine ball, reactive training)
Notes: January 2025; for pars interarticularis stress fracture / spondylolysis / spondylolisthesis; lumbar extension is the critical precaution Phase I; FABQ and STarT Back Tool for chronic; side bridge 80 seconds required for Phase II criteria; prone DL raise >30 sec and supine DL lowering <70 degrees for Phase III

WB Status: WBAT; lumbar support and assistive device for ambulation if indicated Phase I
Key Restrictions: Avoid prolonged sitting, bending, lifting, and repetitive twisting; lumbar support Phase I; assistive device if indicated; frequent position changes; reduce/D/C lumbar support Phase II; return to gym Phase III
Timeline / Phases: 4 phases: Phase I (0-6 wks, 6-8 visits — lumbar support, STM, joint mob, cat/camel, local TA/MF core, modified bridge, sit-to-stand, aquatics/NuStep/recumbent bike/treadmill), Phase II (6-12 wks, 6 visits — dead bug, bird dog, closed chain: squats/lunges/step-ups, anti-rotation, clamshell, balance, thread the needle), Phase III (12-16 wks, 2-4 visits — squat/carry progressions, single-leg pulldowns, PNF diagonals, progressive cardio), Phase IV (16+ wks, optional — sport/recreational specific with gradual loading)
Notes: January 2025; for lumbar spondylosis and degenerative disc changes; similar structure to stenosis but without forced extension precaution; Oswestry Questionnaire; FABQ and STarT Back Tool for chronic cases; discharge: pain-free ranges of all lumbar motions and pre-injury performance without pain

WB Status: NWB surgical arm
Key Restrictions: No lifting/pushing/pulling/forceful gripping; short arm cast or thumb spica until week 6-8 per surgeon; no resistive strengthening, sustained gripping, or WB until after 12 weeks postop
Timeline / Phases: Phase 1 (0-2 wks): postop splint, elevation, finger AROM; Phase 2 (2-6 wks): short arm cast, dexterity tasks, thumb IP ROM; Phase 3 (6-12 wks): wrist 4-way AROM from week 6-8, light grip from week 8-10, progressive wrist strengthening from week 10; full ADLs at week 12
Notes: For scapholunate instability; volar wrist vs. long arm elbow splint depending on surgeon; wrist orthosis progressive weaning from week 8-10

WB Status: NWB initially
Key Restrictions: No lifting/pushing/pulling initially; no resistance strengthening until 4-6 weeks; avoid instrument-assisted scar mobilization directly over carpal tunnel
Timeline / Phases: Phase 1 (0-2 wks): optional volar wrist splint, elevation, finger AROM; Phase 2 (2-6 wks): median nerve glides, soft tissue mobilization, scar management, desensitization; Phase 3 (6-12 wks): progressive UE strengthening, continued nerve glides, address posture/movement patterns
Notes: Splint use at surgeon discretion; padded glove or sleeve for comfort; median nerve glides; posture and nerve compression/traction education

Surgery Type: Cubital Tunnel Release — Anterior Subcutaneous Transposition
WB Status: NWB surgical UE
Key Restrictions: No lifting/pushing/pulling/forceful gripping; no resistance strengthening until 6 weeks postop
Timeline / Phases: Phase 1 (0-2 wks): postop elbow splint, elevation, finger AROM; Phase 2 (2-6 wks): ulnar nerve glides, scar management, AROM elbow/wrist/forearm, desensitization; Phase 3 (6-12 wks): progressive UE strengthening, intrinsic strengthening, scapular stabilization, rotator cuff strengthening
Notes: Ulnar nerve glides throughout; padded elbow sleeve; intrinsic and dexterity emphasis; address compensatory posture patterns

WB Status: NWB surgical UE
Key Restrictions: No lifting/pushing/pulling; no resistance strengthening until 4-6 weeks; avoid repetitive full elbow flexion and shoulder horizontal ABD in Phase 3; may use soft elbow flexion blocking splint at night
Timeline / Phases: Phase 1 (0-2 wks): optional elbow splint at surgeon discretion, finger AROM; Phase 2 (2-6 wks): ulnar nerve glides, AROM elbow/wrist/shoulder; Phase 3 (6-12 wks): progressive UE strengthening, intrinsic and scapular stabilization, avoid repetitive full elbow flexion initially
Notes: May continue soft elbow flexion blocking splint at night as needed; ulnar nerve glides throughout; splint use at surgeon discretion

WB Status: NWB initially
Key Restrictions: Thumb spica postop splint; no resistance strengthening until 6 weeks; avoid 1st dorsal wrist compartment irritation during strengthening
Timeline / Phases: Phase 1 (0-2 wks): thumb spica splint, finger/elbow AROM; Phase 2 (2-6 wks): thumb spica WHFO orthosis, wrist 4-way AROM, thumb opposition/joint blocking, radial nerve glides, begin weaning at week 4; Phase 3 (6-12 wks): wrist/forearm and grip strengthening, continue radial nerve glides
Notes: Radial nerve glides; 1st dorsal compartment tendon gliding; thumb CMC/MCP/IP ROM; night orthosis optional for relative rest

WB Status: NWB surgical arm
Key Restrictions: No active elbow flexion; nothing heavier than a cell phone initially; no resistance strengthening until 6 weeks; hinged elbow orthosis limiting extension (wk 2: 75 deg, wk 3: 60 deg, wk 4: 45 deg, wk 5: 30 deg, wk 6: full) worn full time including sleep
Timeline / Phases: Phase 1 (0-2 wks): posterior long arm splint; Phase 2 (2-6 wks): hinged EO full time with progressive extension limit; Phase 3 (6-12 wks): gravity-resisted elbow flexion, progressive resistance from week 8 (1-3 lbs, then beyond 3 lbs from week 10); Phase 4 (12-16 wks): return to full ADLs excluding heavy lifting
Notes: 4 phases; hinged EO worn including sleep; LABCN neuropathy monitoring; heavy manual labor restricted beyond 12-16 weeks

WB Status: NWB surgical UE; NWB for intra-articular fractures until week 10-12 minimum
Key Restrictions: No lifting/pushing/pulling; no resistance strengthening until 6 weeks; NWB for intra-articular fractures until 10-12 weeks with surgeon clearance; avoid forceful wrist manipulation; avoid EDC compensatory activity for wrist extension
Timeline / Phases: Phase 1 (0-2 wks): volar wrist vs. Muenster splint; Phase 2 (2-6 wks): wrist orthosis full time, AROM wrist/forearm from week 4; Phase 3 (6-8 wks): submax isometric then progressive wrist/forearm strengthening, begin removing orthosis for light sedentary ADLs; Phase 4 (8-12 wks): wean orthosis, return to activity excluding heavy lifting
Notes: 4 phases; Muenster splint option at surgeon discretion; avoid EDC compensation; intra-articular fractures NWB until week 10-12 minimum

WB Status: NWB initially
Key Restrictions: Protect wounds; avoid vigorous use or WB through surgical hand until wound healed; manage scar contracture; night positioning orthosis for 2-3 months postop
Timeline / Phases: Phase 1 (0-2 wks): volar wrist/hand splint maintaining finger extension, AROM unimmobilized joints; Phase 2 (2-4 wks): finger extension orthosis, wound care, AROM with joint blocking for DIP/hook flexion; Phase 3 (4-8 wks): progressive grip and pinch strengthening, dexterity, night orthosis for 2-3 months
Notes: Night orthosis for finger extension positioning maintained 2-3 months postop; LMB dynamic PIP extension orthosis for persistent PIP contractures <35 deg once wound healed; joint blocking for DIP and hook fisting; wound may heal by secondary intention

WB Status: NWB surgical UE
Key Restrictions: Nothing heavier than a cell phone; no pushing up from chair with surgical arm; no resistance strengthening until 8 weeks; max 1-2 lbs resistance; LIFETIME lifting limit <5 lbs
Timeline / Phases: Phase 1 (0-2 wks): posterior long arm splint, finger AROM; Phase 2 (2-6 wks): hinged or custom static EO vs. sling, AROM elbow/wrist/forearm; Phase 3 (6-12 wks): resistance up to 1-2 lbs from week 8, functional activities within lifetime lifting restriction
Notes: LIFETIME lifting restriction <5 lbs; accept elbow flexion contracture <20° as outcome goal; hinged EO full time including sleep; no resistance strengthening before 8 weeks

WB Status: NWB surgical UE
Key Restrictions: No valgus stress to elbow; no lifting/pushing/pulling; no UCL-stressing resistance until 6 weeks; hinged EO (wk 2: 30-100°, wk 4: 10-120°, wk 6: full ROM) full time including sleep
Timeline / Phases: Phase 1 (0-2 wks): postop splint elbow/wrist immobilized; Phase 2 (2-6 wks): hinged EO with progressive ROM, grip and wrist/forearm AROM, isometrics wrist/shoulder/biceps/triceps from week 4; Phase 3 (6-12 wks): wean EO, progressive wrist curls/biceps/triceps (start 1 lb), shoulder strengthening
Notes: Hinged EO full time including sleep; progressive ROM: wk2 30-100°, wk4 10-120°, wk6 full; shoulder strengthening with gravity resistance initially; no valgus stress throughout recovery

WB Status: NWB surgical arm
Key Restrictions: No valgus stress; no lifting/pushing/pulling; no UCL-stressing resistance until 6 weeks; hinged EO (wk 2: 30-100°, wk 4: 10-120°, wk 6: full ROM) full time including sleep
Timeline / Phases: Phase 1 (0-2 wks): postop splint; Phase 2 (2-6 wks): hinged EO, isometric wrist/shoulder from wk 2, progressive resistance wrist/biceps/shoulder from wk 4; Phase 3 (6-14 wks): wean EO, Thrower's Ten, 2-hand plyos wk 8, 1-arm plyos wk 10, interval hitting wk 12-14; Week 16: interval throwing program
Notes: Thrower's Ten program initiated Phase 3; 2-handed plyometrics from week 8 progressing to 1-arm; interval hitting week 12-14; interval throwing program (long toss) week 16; imagery for return to sport during Phase 2

Surgery Type: Extensor Tendon Mallet Finger — Zone I-II Non-Operative
WB Status: No surgical restrictions; light ADLs with DIP extension orthosis at all times
Key Restrictions: DIP orthosis in 0° to slight hyperextension worn without fail at ALL times including hygiene; if removed, DIP must be manually held in full extension; no composite or hook fisting uninvolved fingers; if extension lag noted, resume full-time immobilization 2 additional weeks; no PROM DIP flexion during Phase 2
Timeline / Phases: Phase 1 (day 1 to 6-8 wks): custom static DIP extension orthosis full time, active FDS straight fisting for uninvolved fingers, passive PIP flexion, light hand use without hook/tight fisting; Phase 2 (6-10 wks): orthosis weaning (wk 1: light AROM; wk 2: 2-3 hrs off:on with night wear; wk 3: wean day, discharge night), composite fisting, hook fisting, intrinsic extension
Notes: Non-surgical; if PIP hyperextension noted, add dorsal blocking component at 20-30° PIP flexion until resolved (typically first 3-4 weeks); monitor skin for irritation; monitor for extensor lag throughout weaning; weaning starts at 6-8 weeks per surgeon

Surgery Type: Extensor Tendon Repair — Zone III Delayed Mobilization
WB Status: NWB surgical UE
Key Restrictions: PIP of involved finger immobilized in full extension without fail; DIP AROM allowed; avoid tight fisting/heavy lifting; limited arc PIP motion begins week 4 only if extension lag <10°; wean during day at 6-8 weeks; FO at night until week 10
Timeline / Phases: Phase 1 (0-10-14 days): postop finger splint PIP in extension; Phase 2 (2-4 wks): static FO PIP full extension full time, DIP AROM; Phase 3 (4-10 wks): limited arc PIP (wk4: 0-30°, wk5: 0-45°, wk6: progressive, wk7: gentle PROM), wean day at 6-8 wks, continue FO at night until week 10
Notes: Zone III = central slip; emphasis on active extension over flexion during exercises; extension lag <10° is progression criteria; PIP FO worn at night until week 10; dexterity and grip strengthening from week 7

Surgery Type: Extensor Tendon Repair — Zone III Early Short Arc Motion
WB Status: NWB surgical UE
Key Restrictions: NOT for patients starting therapy at or beyond postop day 7; requires surgeon confirmation of central slip repair without lateral band involvement; do not start if significant edema/stiffness; PIP in extension between sessions; progressive arc limits per week; extension lag <5° to progress
Timeline / Phases: Phase 1 (0-5-7 days): postop PIP extension splint; Phase 2 (5-7 days to 6 wks): short arc motion (SAM) with wrist 30° flexion/MCP extension, template limits (day5-7: 30°, wk2: 45°, wk3: 60°, wk4: 75°, wk5: 90°, wk6+: as tolerated), PIP in extension between sessions; Phase 3 (6-10 wks): progress PIP flexion, PROM from week 7, wean day at 6-8 weeks, FO at night until week 10
Notes: Must initiate by postop day 7; SAM performed with wrist in 30° flexion and MCP in extension; extension lag <5° to progress arc; FO full time between sessions and at night until week 10; emphasis on active extension over flexion

Surgery Type: Extensor Tendon Repair — Zone IV Delayed Mobilization
WB Status: NWB surgical UE
Key Restrictions: PIP and DIP immobilized in full extension without fail; avoid tight fisting until week 10+; limited arc motion begins week 4 only if extension lag <10°; wean day at 6-8 weeks; FO at night until week 10
Timeline / Phases: Phase 1 (0-10-14 days): postop finger splint PIP+DIP in extension; Phase 2 (2-4 wks): static FO PIP+DIP full extension full time; Phase 3 (4-10 wks): limited arc PIP+DIP (wk4: 0-30°, wk5: 0-45°, wk6: progressive, wk7: PROM + dexterity + grip strengthening), wean day 6-8 wks, FO night until week 10
Notes: Zone IV = dorsum of hand; differs from Zone III in that both PIP and DIP are immobilized; extension lag <10° progression criteria; dexterity and progressive grip strengthening from week 7; no heavy lifting until week 10+

Surgery Type: Extensor Tendon Repair — Zones V-VI Delayed Mobilization
WB Status: NWB surgical UE
Key Restrictions: WHFO (wrist ≥15° extension, MCP in extension allowing IP flexion) full time including sleep; avoid concurrent wrist and finger flexion; no resistance strengthening until 6 weeks; orthosis worn full time except hygiene from week 3
Timeline / Phases: Phase 1 (0-2 wks): volar wrist/finger extension splint; Phase 2 (2-4 wks): WHFO full time, AROM IP of involved digit, proximal tendon gliding from week 3; Phase 3 (4-6 wks): WHFO full time, tendon glides (hook/straight/composite/intrinsic extension) from week 5, AROM wrist; Phase 4 (6+ wks): wean orthosis, resist grip from week 7, resistance to repair from week 8, night orthosis until week 8
Notes: 4 phases; Zones V-VI = MCP level / dorsal hand; WHFO wrist ≥15° extension + MCP extension with P1 block forearm-based; additional full digit support attachment for night; edema glove and compression sleeve throughout; avoid end-range wrist flexion during hygiene

Surgery Type: Extensor Tendon Repair — Zones V-VI Relative Motion / Early Motion
WB Status: NWB surgical arm
Key Restrictions: NOT for EDC, EIP, or EDM tendon injuries (use delayed protocol); MCP of involved digit in 20-30° greater extension than adjacent digits at all times; avoid concurrent digit and wrist flexion; no resistance strengthening until 6 weeks; relative motion orthosis full time including sleep
Timeline / Phases: Phase 1 (0-2 wks): volar splint, elevation; Phase 2 (2-4 wks): relative motion orthosis (RMO) full time, composite fisting with RMO in place, place-and-hold MCP/PIP/DIP extension, dexterity; Phase 3 (4-6 wks): continue RMO full time, full hook fisting with MCP extension emphasis; Phase 4 (6+ wks): wean RMO with extension lag monitoring, RMO at night 2-4 more weeks
Notes: 4 phases; NOT for EDC/EIP/EDM injuries; two orthosis options per surgeon: RMO only or RMO + wrist orthosis (WHO); may discontinue WHO in Phase 3 while maintaining RMO; MCP extension emphasis during hook fisting for scar adhesions

Surgery Type: Flexor Pollicis Longus (FPL) Repair — Early Active Place and Hold
WB Status: NWB surgical arm
Key Restrictions: Do not initiate if significant edema/stiffness present; no end-range thumb extension; no resistance strengthening until 8-10 weeks; WHFO dorsal block (wrist neutral to 20° flexion, thumb MCP 15° flexion, IP 30° flexion) full time including sleep; exercises every 1-2 waking hours
Timeline / Phases: Phase 1 (0-3-5 days): thumb spica IP in extension; Phase 2 (3-9 days to 4 wks): WHFO dorsal block, PROM warmup then place-and-hold exercises; Phase 3 (4-6 wks): active tenodesis (wrist flex/thumb ext ↔ wrist ext/thumb flex), gentle thumb extension; Phase 4 (6-12 wks): discontinue orthosis, progressive resistance from week 9
Notes: 4 phases; PROM always performed slowly as warmup before active exercises; place-and-hold: passively flex thumb/extend wrist, actively hold and extend wrist; active MCP motion limited to 30-40°; passive thumb extension not beyond orthosis limit

Surgery Type: Flexor Tendon Repair — Zones I-IV Delayed Mobilization
WB Status: NWB surgical UE
Key Restrictions: Use when therapy delayed >10 days postop OR significant edema/stiffness present; no active finger flexion; no composite wrist+finger extension; do not use surgical hand for self-care; no resistance strengthening until 8-10 weeks; WHFO dorsal block full time including sleep
Timeline / Phases: Phase 1 (0-3-5 days): dorsal block splint; Phase 2 (3-9 days to 4 wks): PROM only (Modified Duran), active PIP extension/intrinsic motion, reverse blocking; Phase 3 (4-6 wks): active tenodesis, AROM wrist, extrinsic flexibility; Phase 4 (6-12 wks): discontinue orthosis, joint blocking, resistance from week 8
Notes: 4 phases; indicated for delayed therapy onset >10 days or high edema; PROM Modified Duran as warmup before all sessions; Zone 1 injuries may limit DIP extension per surgeon; may use relative flexion/extension orthosis for lag or PIP contractures in Phase 4

Surgery Type: Flexor Tendon Repair — Zones I-IV Early Active Place and Hold
WB Status: NWB surgical UE
Key Restrictions: NOT for patients starting therapy at or beyond postop day 10; requires ≥4-strand repair and surgeon confirmation; do not start if significant edema/stiffness; no composite wrist+finger extension; no resistance until 8-10 weeks; WHFO dorsal block full time including sleep; exercises every 1-2 waking hours
Timeline / Phases: Phase 1 (0-3-5 days): dorsal block splint; Phase 2 (3-9 days to 4 wks): PROM (Modified Duran) + place-and-hold exercises, active PIP extension; Phase 3 (4-6 wks): progress place-and-hold to active tenodesis, AROM wrist; Phase 4 (6-12 wks): discontinue orthosis, joint blocking, resistance from week 8
Notes: 4 phases; requires ≥4-strand repair; PROM Modified Duran warmup always; place-and-hold: passively flex fingers/extend wrist, actively hold; active MCP motion limited to 30-40°; if persistent lag, may wean orthosis at week 5 per physician; exercises every 1-2 waking hours

Surgery Type: Flexor Tendon Repair — Zones I-IV Early True Active Flexion (St. John)
WB Status: NWB surgical UE
Key Restrictions: "You can move the hand, but you cannot use it"; NOT for patients starting therapy ≥day 10; requires ≥4-strand repair; no composite wrist+finger extension; no resistance until 8-10 weeks; WHFO dorsal block full time including sleep
Timeline / Phases: Phase 1 (0-3-5 days): dorsal block splint; Phase 2 (3-9 days): PROM + true active half-fisting initiating from DIP joints; Phase 3 (10 days to 4 wks): synergistic tenodesis motion, may transition to Manchester Short Splint (MCP 30-40° flexion/wrist dorsal component limiting extension to 45°); Phase 4 (4-6 wks): AROM wrist, extrinsic flexibility; Phase 5 (6-12 wks): discontinue orthosis, resistance from week 8
Notes: 5 phases; St. John protocol; true active flexion initiating from DIP (not MCP); active MCP limited to 30-40°; may progress to 3/4 fist in Phase 3; Manchester Short Splint transition possible at 2-4 weeks; PROM Modified Duran warmup always; if lag persists, may wean at week 5 per physician

Surgery Type: Lateral Epicondyle (Tennis Elbow) Debridement with Tendon Repair
WB Status: NWB surgical UE
Key Restrictions: No lifting/pushing/pulling; no resistance strengthening until 6 weeks; cock-up wrist orthosis (WHO) in 30-40° extension full time until week 6; protect tendon repair throughout Phases 1-2
Timeline / Phases: Phase 1 (0-2 wks): long arm elbow/wrist splint; Phase 1b (2-4 wks): cock-up WHO 30-40°, elbow AROM; Phase 2 (4-6 wks): continue WHO, wrist/forearm AROM, radial nerve glides, scapular stabilization, imagery for RTS; Phase 3 (6-12 wks): wean orthosis, progressive wrist/forearm and grip strengthening, radial nerve glides, low-level RTS activities
Notes: Radial nerve glides; scapular stabilization (prone horizontal ABD, full can, serratus wall slides); wrist orthosis at night for relative rest/positioning until Phase 3; low-level RTS activities in Phase 3 per symptoms

Surgery Type: Lateral Epicondyle (Tennis Elbow) Debridement without Tendon Repair
WB Status: NWB surgical UE
Key Restrictions: No lifting/pushing/pulling; no resistance strengthening until 6 weeks; cock-up wrist orthosis (WHO) in 30-40° extension full time until acute pain subsides (2-4 weeks) then wean
Timeline / Phases: Phase 1 (0-2 wks): long arm elbow/wrist splint, elevation; Phase 2 (2-6 wks): cock-up WHO 30-40°, wrist 4-way and forearm AROM, elbow AROM, radial nerve glides, scapular stabilization; Phase 3 (6-12 wks): wean WHO, progressive wrist/forearm and grip strengthening, radial nerve glides, low-level RTS activities
Notes: Radial nerve glides; scapular stabilization (prone horizontal ABD, full can, serratus wall slides); earlier WHO weaning than with-repair protocol; low-level RTS activities in Phase 3 per symptoms

Surgery Type: MCP Joint Arthroplasty with EDC Centralization
WB Status: NWB surgical UE
Key Restrictions: No lifting/pushing/pulling; WHFO (wrist neutral to slight extension, MCP in extension allowing IP flexion); no ulnar deviation of digits; no resistance strengthening until 8 weeks; no composite wrist and finger flexion until week 8
Timeline / Phases: Phase 1 (0-2 wks): volar wrist/hand splint; Phase 2 (2-6 wks): WHFO full time, IP AROM, week 4: short arc 0-30° MCP with hook emphasis, AROM wrist; Phase 3 (6+ wks): wean orthosis during day/continue at night until 8 weeks, composite fist and wrist flex from week 8, grip strengthening from week 8; RA may need long-term anti-ulnar deviation support
Notes: For MCP arthroplasty WITH EDC centralization; RA patients may need long-term soft neoprene anti-ulnar deviation orthosis; OA patients wean orthosis as tolerated; additional full digit support attachment for night especially if multiple digits; short arc MCP ROM at week 4 with emphasis on extension in hook position

WB Status: NWB surgical UE
Key Restrictions: No lifting/pushing/pulling; no ulnar deviation of digits; MCP in 0° to prevent active extension lag; RA: WHFO full time; OA: hand-based HFO full time; no resistance strengthening until 8 weeks
Timeline / Phases: Phase 1 (0-2 wks): volar wrist/hand splint; Phase 2 (2-6 wks): WHFO (RA) or HFO (OA), progressive active MCP flexion/extension, tendon glides, dexterity; OA at wk 4-6: may remold to functional flexion (30-45°) if stiff and lag <15°, begin removing for light ADLs; Phase 3 (6+ wks): wean orthosis, grip from week 8, RA night orthosis until week 8
Notes: Use when NO additional soft tissue repair to centralize EDC; RA uses forearm-based WHFO, OA uses hand-based HFO; buddy strap support when using hand without orthosis; OA may remold to functional MCP flexion at 4-6 weeks if flexion stiff and active extension lag <15°

Surgery Type: Medial Epicondyle (Golfer's Elbow) Debridement with Tendon Repair
WB Status: NWB surgical UE
Key Restrictions: No lifting/pushing/pulling; no resistance strengthening until 6 weeks; wrist neutral orthosis (WHO) full time; protect tendon repair throughout Phases 1-2
Timeline / Phases: Phase 1 (0-2 wks): posterior long arm elbow/wrist splint; Phase 1b (2-4 wks): wrist neutral WHO, elbow AROM; Phase 2 (4-6 wks): continue WHO, wrist 4-way/forearm AROM, ulnar nerve glides, scapular stabilization, imagery for RTS; Phase 3 (6-12 wks): wean WHO, progressive wrist/forearm and grip strengthening, ulnar nerve glides, low-level RTS activities
Notes: Wrist neutral orthosis (not extension-biased like lateral epicondyle); ulnar nerve glides; scapular stabilization (prone horizontal ABD, full can, serratus wall slides); wrist orthosis at night for relative rest/positioning; low-level RTS in Phase 3

Surgery Type: Medial Epicondyle (Golfer's Elbow) Debridement without Tendon Repair
WB Status: NWB surgical UE
Key Restrictions: No lifting/pushing/pulling; no resistance strengthening until 6 weeks; wrist neutral orthosis (WHO) full time until acute surgical pain subsides (2-4 weeks)
Timeline / Phases: Phase 1 (0-2 wks): posterior long arm elbow/wrist splint; Phase 2 (2-6 wks): wrist neutral WHO, wrist 4-way/forearm AROM, elbow AROM, ulnar nerve glides, scapular stabilization, imagery; Phase 3 (6-12 wks): wean WHO, progressive wrist/forearm and grip strengthening, nerve glides, low-level RTS activities
Notes: Wrist neutral orthosis (not extension-biased); ulnar nerve glides in Phase 2; earlier WHO weaning than with-repair protocol; scapular stabilization; low-level RTS per symptoms in Phase 3

WB Status: NWB surgical UE
Key Restrictions: No resistance strengthening until 8 weeks; PIP flexion typically limited to 80-90° to prevent excessive prosthesis stress; monitor for active PIP extension lag throughout; medial/lateral joint support required; night PIP+DIP extension orthosis to prevent lag and flexion contracture
Timeline / Phases: Phase 1 (0-2 wks): finger splint; Phase 2 (2-6 wks): FO medial/lateral PIP support in full extension full time (with buddy taping for hygiene), tendon glides, joint blocking, intrinsic extension and reverse blocking, dexterity; Phase 3 (6+ wks): buddy straps for medial/lateral protection, PROM for functional PIP flexion (up to 80-90°), dexterity, grip strengthening from week 8; night PIP+DIP extension orthosis continues
Notes: PIP flexion limited to 80-90° to protect prosthesis; night PIP+DIP extension orthosis to prevent extension lag and flexion contracture; buddy taping for medial/lateral protection during ADLs; monitor for active extension lag; DIP may or may not be included in orthosis depending on stability

WB Status: NWB surgical UE until 12 weeks minimum
Key Restrictions: NWB until 12 weeks; no compression/distraction activities (bench press, pullups, pushups) until 6 months postop; no resistance elbow strengthening until 6 weeks; forearm position during rehab depends on collateral repair: LCL repair = maintain pronation limit supination to neutral x3-4 wks; MCL repair = maintain supination limit pronation to neutral x3-4 wks; both = neutral midrange
Timeline / Phases: Phase 1 (0-2 wks): posterior long arm splint; Phase 2 (2-6 wks): hinged EO blocking terminal 30-60° extension progressively, protected forearm position per collateral repair status, AROM wrist, light grip at week 4; Phase 3 (6-12 wks): wean hinged EO (6-8 weeks), wrist/elbow isometrics and 1-5 lbs from wk 6-8, resume ADLs wk 8-12; avoid >15 lbs and compression/distraction until 6 months
Notes: NWB until 12 weeks per surgeon; avoid bench press/pullups/pushups until 6 months postop; hinged EO increases extension ROM by 15°/week or 10°/5 days; forearm position depends on LCL/MCL/both repair status; no lifting >15 lbs until 8-12 weeks minimum

WB Status: NWB surgical UE
Key Restrictions: No lifting/pushing/pulling; no resistance strengthening until 6 weeks; wrist orthosis in 30° extension
Timeline / Phases: Phase 1 (0-2 wks): posterior long arm elbow/wrist splint; Phase 2 (2-6 wks): WHO in 30° extension, soft tissue mobilization through neural pathway, radial nerve glides, wrist 4-way/forearm AROM, desensitization if hypersensitive; Phase 3 (6-12 wks): wean WHO, progressive UE strengthening, scapular stabilization/RTC, radial nerve glides, address dysfunctional posture
Notes: Radial nerve glides throughout; WHO in 30° wrist extension (differs from cubital tunnel which uses elbow orthosis); posture and nerve compression/traction education; desensitization if hypersensitive at surgical site; myofascial mobilization for scar in Phase 3

WB Status: NWB surgical UE
Key Restrictions: No lifting/pushing/pulling; involved MCP in full extension or relative extension at all times; no full composite fisting of involved finger; no resistance strengthening until 6 weeks; orthosis full time; avoid tight composite fisting with forearm rotation until week 8-10
Timeline / Phases: Phase 1 (0-2 wks): volar wrist/hand splint; Phase 2 (2-8 wks): hand-based P1 block or relative motion orthosis (MCP in extension) full time, AROM hook fisting, composite fisting with RMO in place, wrist 4-way/forearm AROM, dexterity; Phase 3 (8-12 wks): wean RMO (until 8-10 weeks), gradual full composite fist after 8-10 weeks, ergonomics education
Notes: Ergonomics education: wider grip tools, grip tape on golf clubs/racquets, pipe insulation on tool handles, frequent movement breaks from prolonged gripping (driving, pickleball, etc.); two orthosis options per surgeon: hand-based P1 block or relative motion orthosis

WB Status: NWB surgical UE
Key Restrictions: No lifting/pushing/pulling; wrist in cast until week 6-8; no resistive strengthening, sustained gripping, WB, or distraction activities until after 12 weeks postop; orthosis continues for demanding/dynamic loading until 16 weeks postop
Timeline / Phases: Phase 1 (0-2 wks): thumb spica splint; Phase 2 (2-6 wks): short arm cast or thumb spica until week 6-8, dexterity tasks, thumb IP ROM; Phase 3 (6-12 wks): wrist neutral orthosis full time, wrist 4-way AROM from wk 6-8, forearm rotation, assisted ROM and light grip (<10 lbs) from wk 8-12, progressive ADLs from wk 12; orthosis continues for demanding activities until wk 16
Notes: More restrictive than Blatt's capsulodesis: wrist immobilized until week 6-8 vs. 6 for Blatt's; orthosis continues for demanding/dynamic loading until 16 weeks (vs. 12 for Blatt's); light grip <10 lbs from week 8-12; no heavy loading until >12 weeks minimum

WB Status: NWB surgical UE
Key Restrictions: No lifting/pushing/pulling; no strengthening until 6 weeks; wrist orthosis full time until week 4
Timeline / Phases: Phase 1 (0-2 wks): volar wrist splint, finger/elbow AROM; Phase 2 (2-6 wks): wrist orthosis full time, wrist 4-way/forearm AROM, AAROM, dexterity; may remove orthosis for light self-care and seated activities from week 4; Phase 3 (6-12 wks): wean orthosis, progressive wrist strengthening, AAROM/PROM
Notes: Simpler protocol than TFCC peripheral repair; no immobilization beyond wrist orthosis; may transition to compression sleeve/wrist wrap during orthosis weaning; scar management as needed

WB Status: NWB surgical UE until 12 weeks minimum
Key Restrictions: No strengthening until 8-10 weeks; immobilization type and duration depends on surgical findings and DRUJ stability; if small repair with stable DRUJ in removable orthosis: short-arc ROM from week 4; NWB until 12 weeks per surgeon
Timeline / Phases: Phase 1 (0-2 wks): posterior long arm elbow/wrist or volar wrist splint; Phase 2 (2-6 wks): wrist orthosis vs. Muenster orthosis vs. cast per surgeon/findings; if stable DRUJ: short-arc wrist/forearm AROM from week 4; Phase 3 (6-12 wks): wean orthosis (6-8 weeks), isometric then isotonic wrist strengthening at 8-10 weeks, light grip <10 lbs; night orthosis until 8-10 weeks
Notes: Confirm with surgeon before initiating ROM — immobilization type and ROM timing depends on DRUJ stability findings; NWB until 12 weeks; longer immobilization and strength restrictions than TFCC debridement; night orthosis until 8-10 weeks

WB Status: NWB surgical UE
Key Restrictions: No lifting/pushing/pulling; grip/pinch with minimal to no resistance; orthosis protection for functional activities; cast 2-3 weeks then HFO or WHFO until 10 weeks; may remove for light self-care from week 8
Timeline / Phases: Phase 1 (0-2 wks): thumb spica splint; Phase 2 (2-10 wks): cast 2-3 weeks then HFO short opponens or WHFO thumb spica, wrist/forearm AROM, thumb CMC/MCP neuromuscular retraining (C's and O's), joint blocking, dexterity, radial nerve glides if SSRN hypersensitivity; Phase 3 (10-12 wks): wean orthosis, progressive wrist strengthening, continued neuromuscular reeducation
Notes: LRTI = Ligament Reconstruction Tendon Interposition; neuromuscular retraining for balanced pinch (C's and O's) and first dorsal interosseous; manual thumb adductor release; SSRN hypersensitivity monitoring; joint conservation education; long-term Comfort Cool CMC orthosis recommended PRN for soft tissue rest

WB Status: NWB surgical UE
Key Restrictions: No lifting/pushing/pulling; grip/pinch with minimal to no resistance; orthosis protection; cast 2-3 weeks then HFO or WHFO until 8 weeks; may remove for light self-care from week 6-8
Timeline / Phases: Phase 1 (0-2 wks): thumb spica splint; Phase 2 (2-8 wks): cast 2-3 weeks then HFO short opponens or WHFO, thumb CMC/MCP neuromuscular retraining (C's and O's), joint blocking, dexterity, radial nerve glides if SSRN hypersensitivity; Phase 3 (8-12 wks): wean orthosis, progressive wrist strengthening, continued neuromuscular reeducation
Notes: Similar to LRTI but shorter Phase 2 (ends at 8 weeks vs. 10 weeks for LRTI); neuromuscular retraining for balanced pinch; SSRN hypersensitivity monitoring; joint conservation education; Comfort Cool CMC orthosis for long-term support PRN

WB Status: NWB surgical UE
Key Restrictions: No 2-point (tip) or 3-point (chuck/tripod) pinch strengthening until 8 weeks; no lifting/pushing/pulling; cast weeks 2-4, custom thumb spica weeks 4-8; residual MCP stiffness expected long-term
Timeline / Phases: Phase 1 (0-2 wks): thumb spica splint; Phase 2 (2-8 wks): cast wk 2-4 then custom hand-based thumb spica wk 4-8, thumb IP AROM, MCP flexion/extension with CMC joint blocking from wk 4-6, balanced pinch/grasp without resistance, gentle passive MCP flexion from wk 6; Phase 3 (8-12 wks): progressive grip/pinch strengthening, wean orthosis, prolonged low-load MCP flexion ROM
Notes: 2-point and 3-point pinch strengthening contraindicated until 8 weeks; cast weeks 2-4 transitions to custom orthosis weeks 4-8; residual MCP stiffness expected long-term; may use neoprene thumb support after orthosis weaning; light sedentary ADLs allowed gradually after week 8

WB Status: NWB surgical UE
Key Restrictions: No lifting/pushing/pulling; no resistance strengthening through ROM until 8 weeks; hinged EO LIMITING FLEXION (not extension): wk 2=45°, wk 3=60°, wk 4=90°, wk 6=120° worn full time including sleep
Timeline / Phases: Phase 1 (0-2 wks): long arm/elbow splint; Phase 2 (2-6 wks): hinged EO limiting elbow flexion progressively, wrist/forearm AROM, isometric elbow flexion, gravity-assisted elbow extension; Phase 3 (6-12 wks): wean hinged EO (6-8 weeks), submax isometric elbow extension at 30/60/90/120° from wk 6, progressive resistance from wk 8, triceps flexibility, radial nerve glides, scapular stabilization
Notes: Key distinction: hinged EO limits FLEXION (opposite of distal biceps repair which limits extension); flexion limits: wk2=45°, wk3=60°, wk4=90°, wk6=120°; in 10° increments increase by 10°/5 days; reference: Kocialkowski et al. 2018

WB Status: NWB initially; functional use within soft tissue tolerance from Phase 2
Key Restrictions: No resistance strengthening until 4-6 weeks; no postop orthosis unless PIP flexion contracture present (then static night extension orthosis or LMB dynamic PIP extension orthosis)
Timeline / Phases: Phase 1 (0-2 wks): NO orthosis, AROM tendon glides, wrist 4-way/forearm/elbow AROM; Phase 2 (2-6 wks): joint blocking, tendon glides with place-and-hold if active lag, dexterity, scar management, desensitization; static night extension or LMB dynamic orthosis only if PIP contracture present; Phase 3 (6-12 wks): gentle strengthening, address persistent PIP contracture with hook fisting and reverse blocking
Notes: No postop splint or orthosis routinely required (unique among UVA protocols); static night extension orthosis or LMB dynamic extension orthosis only for PIP flexion contracture; ergonomics education to prevent trigger fingers in uninvolved digits; padded glove or hand sleeve for comfort PRN

WB Status: NWB surgical UE
Key Restrictions: No passive forearm rotation; limit forearm pronation to midrange until 6 weeks; no resistance strengthening until 8 weeks; cast until week 3-4; WHO in neutral until week 8; WHO at night until week 10-12; soft tissue repair stabilizes ulna from translation
Timeline / Phases: Phase 1 (0-2 wks): volar wrist or posterior long arm splint; Phase 2 (2-8 wks): cast until wk 3-4 then WHO neutral full time, wrist flex/ext midrange from wk 4, forearm rotation from full supination to midrange pronation; PROM from wk 8, resistive strengthening from wk 10; Phase 3 (8-12 wks): wean WHO during day, continue WHO at night until wk 10-12, progressive wrist/forearm strengthening, PROM
Notes: Average functional ROM post-Darrach: pronation/supination 85/78°, flexion/extension 41/45°, radial/ulnar deviation 14/19° (Grawe et al. 2012); soft tissue repair stabilizes ulna from translation; limit pronation to midrange until 6 weeks; long-term night orthosis as needed

WB Status: NWB surgical UE; no WB until surgeon clearance
Key Restrictions: No lifting/pushing/pulling; wrist immobilized in short arm cast until fusion confirmed on X-ray (typically 10-12 weeks); no resistance strengthening until fusion confirmed; ROM deficits expected long-term
Timeline / Phases: Phase 1 (0-10-14 days): volar wrist splint; Phase 2 (2 weeks to 10-12 weeks until fusion confirmed): short arm cast, may transition to custom static WHO or Exos semi-custom WHO, PROM fingers, light hand use within cast/orthosis; Phase 3 (10-16 weeks): wean orthosis, AROM wrist 4-way/forearm, progressive wrist/forearm strengthening, PROM 2 weeks after initiating AROM; may progress ADLs from wk 10-12
Notes: Wrist immobilized until fusion confirmed on X-ray (typically 10-12 weeks); ROM deficits expected long-term; recovery may take several months to >1 year; may transition from cast to custom WHO or Exos per surgeon; no WB until cleared by surgeon

WB Status: NWB surgical UE; no WB
Key Restrictions: No strengthening until 6 weeks; no WB; avoid composite wrist/digit flexibility exercises (prevents extrinsic stretching); avoid forceful wrist manipulation; heavy lifting contraindicated long-term; avoid EDC compensatory activity for wrist extension
Timeline / Phases: Phase 1 (0-2 wks): volar wrist splint; Phase 2 (2-4 wks): short arm cast or full-time wrist orthosis, address finger lag, light dexterity; Phase 3 (4-8 wks): wrist 4-way AROM, isolated wrist extension (avoid EDC compensation), forearm rotation, isometric wrist from wk 6, orthosis for light sedentary ADLs from wk 6; Phase 4 (8-12 wks): wean orthosis, return to full ADLs excluding heavy lifting
Notes: 4 phases; avoid EDC compensatory activity for wrist extension; avoid composite wrist/digit flexibility; heavy lifting (e.g., construction) excluded long-term; gravity-assisted wrist motion; no forceful wrist manipulation to regain ROM; compression sleeve/wrist wrap for comfort during weaning

WB Status: NWB surgical UE
Key Restrictions: No lifting/pushing/pulling; no resistance strengthening or PROM until 8 weeks; LIFETIME restrictions: permanently avoid repeated forceful activities (hammering), heavy manual work, and contact sports
Timeline / Phases: Phase 1 (0-2 wks): volar wrist splint; Phase 2 (2-6 wks): rigid WHO (custom thermoplastic or Exos semi-custom), AROM wrist 4-way/forearm, light 2-handed functional tasks, dexterity; Phase 3 (7-12 wks): wean orthosis, PROM and progressive wrist strengthening from wk 8; target ROM: extension 40°, flexion 20°, radial deviation 10°, ulnar deviation 15°; functional activities within lifetime precautions from wk 12
Notes: LIFETIME restrictions on heavy manual work, repeated forceful activities, contact sports; target ROM per Green's Operative Hand Surgery 8th Ed.; ROM deficits expected long-term; recovery takes several months to >1 year; long-term wrist orthosis PRN for sleep, activity, and soft tissue rest; UVA uses Anika Wrist Motion system

WB Status: 50% WB with crutches for 6 weeks; progress to FWB after 6 weeks
Key Restrictions: Brace 0-90° for 6 weeks (unlocked at all times after nerve block resolves, removable for hygiene and therapy); no forced flexion; 50% WB for 6 weeks
Timeline / Phases: Phase 1 (0-6 wks): brace 0-90°, 50% WB, quad recruitment, SLR, 4-way hip, stationary bike, aquatic therapy; Phase 2 (6-10 wks): progress to FWB, full AROM, CKC strengthening, lunges, leg press, step-downs, balance; Phase 3 (10+ wks): plyometrics, running progression, cutting and agility exercises
Notes: Distinct from knee arthroscopy: 50% WB and brace 0-90° for 6 weeks with no forced flexion; brace unlocked at all times after nerve block; aquatic therapy may begin after first post-op visit; running and plyometrics begin at 10+ weeks

WB Status: 50% WB weeks 0-2; WBAT in brace weeks 2-4; progress to FWB
Key Restrictions: Brace 0-90° weeks 0-4; no ambulation without brace first 4 weeks; avoid lateralization of patella; avoid AAROM knee extension with significant quad atrophy or articular cartilage injury; ROM 0-110° until week 8, 0-120° by week 10
Timeline / Phases: Phase 1 (0-6 wks): 50% WB then WBAT, brace 0-90°, quad sets/NMES, SLR 4-way, hamstring activation, balance as tolerated; Phase 2 (7-10 wks): normalize gait, ROM 0-110° until wk 8 then 0-120° by wk 10, leg press, FSU progression, stationary bike; Phase 3 (11-18+ wks): maintain full ROM, running initiation, bilateral plyometrics; Phase 4 (19-24 wks): advanced strengthening, cutting/deceleration with MD clearance; Discharge criteria: 85% LSI isokinetics at 180/300°/sec, hop test >85% LSI
Notes: 4 phases; avoid patellar lateralization throughout; ROM targets: 0-110° by week 8, 0-120° by week 10; isokinetic and hop testing at discharge; medical clearance required for RTS; lateral jump progression (broad jumps, single-leg landings) in Phase 4

Surgery Type: MPFL Reconstruction / Tibial Tubercle Osteotomy / Trochleoplasty
WB Status: 50% WB for 6 weeks (more conservative than MPFL alone)
Key Restrictions: 50% WB for 6 weeks; no ambulation without brace first 6 weeks; brace 0-70° weeks 0-2, 0-90° weeks 2-4, unlock after week 4; avoid lateralization of patella; ROM 0-110° until week 8, 0-120° by week 10, full by 12 weeks
Timeline / Phases: Phase 1 (0-6 wks): 50% WB, brace 0-70° progressing to 0-90° then unlocked, quad sets/NMES, SLR 4-way, hamstring activation; Phase 2 (7-10 wks): normalize gait, ROM 0-110° to 0-120° to full, leg press (bilateral), FSU, stationary bike short crank progressing as ROM allows; Phase 3 (11-18+ wks): full ROM by 12 wks, running initiation, bilateral plyometrics; Phase 4 (19-24 wks): advanced strengthening, cutting with MD clearance; Criteria: 85% LSI isokinetics, hop test >85%
Notes: Combined MPFL + TTO + Trochleoplasty; more conservative than MPFL alone: 50% WB for 6 weeks (vs. 4 weeks), brace starts at 0-70° (vs. 0-90°); stationary bike uses short crank progressing to standard crank (needs 115° flexion sitting); full ROM target by 12 weeks; same discharge criteria as MPFL alone

WB Status: 50% WB for 6 weeks
Key Restrictions: 50% WB for 6 weeks; brace locked at 0° weeks 0-2 (may unlock only for prone ROM under PT supervision); brace unlocked 0-90° weeks 2-6; ALL ROM exercises must be performed prone or side-lying for first 6 weeks; no active strengthening with knee flexion; prevent anterior/posterior tibial translation and rotation
Timeline / Phases: Phase 1 (0-6 wks): brace locked 0° then unlocked 0-90°, 50% WB, quad sets/SLR, NO active strengthening with knee flexion, patella mobilizations, prone ROM exercises, Russian stim in extension; Phase 2 (6-10 wks): wean crutches to FWB, prone flexion 120°+, CKC exercises, balance, step-ups; Phase 3 (10-16 wks): progress to full ROM, HS strengthening prone, advance quad; Phase 4 (4-6 months): plyometrics, walk-jog; Phase 5 (6+ months): return to sport/work
Notes: Critical: all ROM exercises prone or side-lying only for first 6 weeks; no active strengthening with knee flexion in Phase 1; prevent tibial translation with ankle weights (increase anterior translation, use cautiously); quad exercises in home program; covers ACL, PCL, with or without MCL, LCL, PLC

WB Status: 50% WB for 6 weeks
Key Restrictions: 50% WB for 6 weeks; brace locked 0° weeks 0-2, unlocked 0-90° weeks 2-6; ALL ROM exercises prone or side-lying only first 6 weeks; NO hamstring isometrics; NO active strengthening with knee flexion; prevent posterior tibial translation and tibial rotation (paramount through 8 weeks); PCL/PLC fixation less sturdy than ACL fixation
Timeline / Phases: Phase 1 (0-6 wks): brace locked 0° then 0-90°, 50% WB, quad sets/SLR, NO HS isometrics, prone ROM only, Russian stim in extension; Phase 2 (6-10 wks): wean crutches to FWB, prone flexion 120°+, CKC, balance, step-ups, lateral stepping around week 8, HS curls 5 lbs max; Phase 3 (10-16 wks): lateral movements (supervised), jogging assessment at 12 wks (if full ROM/normalized gait/strength), isokinetics; Phase 4 (4-6 months): plyometrics, walk-jog, sport-specific supervised; Phase 5 (6+ months): full return, unrestricted lower body lifting ~10-12 months for athletes
Notes: NO hamstring isometrics in Phases 1-2 (protects PCL/PLC); ROM prone/side-lying only first 6 weeks; PCL/PLC fixation less sturdy than ACL — posterior translation prevention paramount through 8 weeks; HS curls limited to 5 lbs max in Phase 2; 10-12 months for full return to sport for athletes; isokinetic testing before full return (quad difference <15%, H:Q ratio 65%+)

WB Status: FWB with brace locked at 0° using crutches for 4 weeks; wean crutches after 6 weeks
Key Restrictions: Brace locked 0° for first 4 weeks (lock to 90° when sitting); no active knee ROM; no active knee extension against gravity; no ambulation without brace for first 6 weeks; heel slides limited to 0-60° weeks 2-4, 0-90° weeks 4-6; avoid deep flexion (squats/lunges) through Phase 3
Timeline / Phases: Phase 1 (0-2 wks): brace locked 0°, no active knee ROM, passive flexion <30°, quad sets, gentle patellar mobilization (medial/lateral only), SLR; Phase 2 (2-6 wks): brace locked 0° still, limit flexion 90°, aqua therapy, CKC, heel slides 0-60° then 0-90°; Phase 3 (6-12 wks): wean crutches/brace, stationary bike, leg press bilateral, wall squats, step-ups/downs, hamstring curls; Phase 4 (12-16 wks): FWB without brace, full ROM, jogging initiation on treadmill, lateral movements; Strength goal: 85-95% of uninvolved leg (isokinetic testing)
Notes: Brace locked 0° for first 4 weeks including sleeping; no active knee extension against gravity; lock brace to 90° when sitting; wean crutches after 6 weeks; no jogging until Phase 4 (12+ weeks); strength goal 85-95% uninvolved leg; covers both quad tendon and patellar tendon repairs

WB Status: 25% WB weeks 0-2; 50% WB weeks 2-8; progress to WBAT after 8 weeks
Key Restrictions: Prolonged protected WB (50% for up to 8 wks); if graft involves patella/trochlea: brace locked in extension for ambulation, unlocked at all other times; physician clearance required before high-impact activities (confirm graft healing on X-ray)
Timeline / Phases: Phase 1 (0-6 wks): brace 0-90° then open to available ROM, 25% then 50% WB, quad/HS/glute strengthening, NMES, SLR 4-ways, stationary bike wks 3-4 low resistance; Phase 2 (6-12 wks): 50% WB until wk 8 then WBAT, discontinue brace at 6 wks, CKC quad/HS, pool program, leg press 0-60°, mini-squats, wall sits; Phase 3 (12-24 wks): FWB without crutches, balance/NM control, BOSU squats/lunges, elliptical/swimming/cycling, functional/sport-specific; Phase 4 (6+ months): sport-specific activities with physician clearance for high-impact (graft healing confirmed on X-ray)
Notes: 4 phases; physician clearance required before any high-impact activities — graft healing must be confirmed on X-ray; if patella/trochlea graft, brace locked in extension for ambulation; 50% WB extended to 8 weeks (longer than typical); pool program begins phase 2; running and impact progression begins Phase 4 only after imaging clearance

Surgery Type: High Tibial Osteotomy / Distal Femoral Osteotomy (HTO/DFO)
WB Status: 25% WB weeks 0-4; medial opening wedge HTO: 50% WB weeks 4-6, WBAT weeks 6-8; FWB without crutches by Phase 3 (8+ weeks)
Key Restrictions: No active hamstring exercises first 2 weeks; brace 0-90° then full ROM; caution with CKC knee flexion >90° early; mini-squats limited to 0-45° at weeks 8-10; brace discontinued at 6 weeks if SLR without extensor lag; cardiovascular conditioning (elliptical/swimming) from 12+ weeks only
Timeline / Phases: Phase 1 (0-4 wks): TTWB to 25% WB, brace 0-90° then open, quad NMES, no active HS first 2 wks, SLR, cycling for ROM (wk 2); Phase 2 (4-8 wks): progressive WB (50% wks 4-6, WBAT wks 6-8), discontinue brace at 6 wks, CKC (mini-squats/wall sits/TKE), OKC HS wk 6+, OKC quad ext wk 7+; Phase 3 (8-16 wks): FWB, mini-squats 0-45° wks 8-10, heel taps/step-ups 6-8" wks 10-12, OKC quad full ROM wks 12-14, cardiovascular from wk 12; Phase 4 (3+ months): running progression, plyometrics and agility month 4+
Notes: No active hamstring exercises for first 2 weeks; WB progression listed for medial opening wedge HTO specifically; mini-squats limited to 0-45° at weeks 8-10; cardiovascular conditioning (elliptical/swimming) from 12+ weeks; plyometrics and agility begin month 4; covers both HTO and DFO

WB Status: WBAT with crutches; wean crutches once ambulating without a limp (goal by week 2)
Key Restrictions: Squats limited to 90° in Phase 2; no jogging until 2-3 months; crutches weeks 0-2 minimum, wean when gait normalized
Timeline / Phases: Phase 1 (0-4 wks): WBAT with crutches, wean by wk 2 if no limp, quad sets, SLR 4-way, heel slides, glute squeezes, patella mobilizations, gentle HS stretch; Phase 2 (4-8 wks): FWB, full ROM, biking, elliptical (wk 6), leg press, CKC TKE, squats to 90° only, step-ups/downs, balance; Phase 3 (8-12 wks): low-impact plyometrics (monitor for reactive edema), kneeling on foam pad, swimming; Phase 4 (12+ wks): return to pre-op activity, running progression; Return to sport: swimming/cycling/golf 4-6 wks, jogging 2-3 months, racquet sports 3-6 months, skiing 6+ months; high impact per MD
Notes: MISHA = partial/unicompartmental knee arthroplasty; WBAT immediately; faster return to function than TKR; kneeling activities encouraged on foam pad (Phase 3); squats limited to 90° in Phase 2; explicit return-to-sport timeline provided; high-impact activities per MD discretion; monitor for reactive edema with plyometrics

WB Status: WB varies by defect type/location: PF single = FWB locked; TF single/multiple = TTWB 20% increasing 10%/week; PF/TF combo = TTWB 20%; most types reach FWB by 6-8 weeks
Key Restrictions: WB and brace protocols individualized by defect location (PF vs TF) and number (single/multiple/combo); ROM starts 0-45° advancing 15°/week; avoid active knee extension against resistance early; avoid excessive load on graft site; physician clearance for high-impact and contact sports (9-12+ months per defect type); emphasis on goal-based rather than time-based progression
Timeline / Phases: Phase 1 (0-3 wks): protect implant, brace 0-30° (locked extension for ambulation/sleep), TTWB-20% WB (TF) or FWB locked (PF), passive ROM 0-45° (CPM encouraged), quad/HS/glute recruitment; Phase 2 (4-6 wks): 50% WB most types, brace 0-45° to 90°, ROM goal 90° by wk 6, recumbent cycling, hydrotherapy; Phase 3 (7-12 wks): gradual transition off crutches/brace, full ROM by wk 9, upright cycling, standing hip strengthening; Phase 4 (3-6 months): FWB off brace, low-impact recreation (cycling/swimming/elliptical), gradual running; Phase 5 (6-9 months): running/agility progression; Phase 6 (9+ months): return to contact sports 9-12+ months depending on defect type
Notes: 6 phases; WB and brace individualized per defect location and number — clinician must follow defect-specific sub-protocol; CPM encouraged in Phase 1; avoid active knee extension against resistance early; avoid full WB without brace/crutches until advanced; return to contact sports: PF single 12+ months, TF single 10-12 months, TF multiple/combo 12+ months; source: MACI Rehabilitation Manual

Surgery Type: General Shoulder Arthroscopy (Debridement / Subacromial Decompression / Distal Clavicle Resection)
WB Status: NWB surgical UE initially; functional use as tolerated with sling discontinued by 1-2 weeks
Key Restrictions: Sling until comfortable (typically 1-2 weeks); avoid horizontal adduction stretching for 6 weeks if distal clavicle resection performed; no conventional weight lifting until 6 weeks
Timeline / Phases: 0-2 wks: sling, RC isometrics (flex/ext/abd/add/IR/ER), scapular exercises, PROM to flex 90°/abd 90°/IR 90°/ER 45°; 2 wks: Theraband/tubing IR/ER with arm abducted 20-30°; 4-6 wks: RC isotonics (prone flexion/abd/ext/scaption), scapular isotonics (shrugs/press-ups/punches/rows), proprioception, PNF D1/D2, isokinetics with 60° block at 30-45° abd; 6+ wks: conventional weight lifting, full ROM isokinetics; RTS criteria: doctor clearance, strength test, no pain with full ROM, throwing program if applicable
Notes: Covers debridement, subacromial decompression, and/or distal clavicle resection; sling typically off by 1-2 weeks; RC isotonics target 6-8 lbs, 5-6 sets of 15-20 reps at 4-6 weeks; isokinetics begin at 4-6 weeks with 60° block, advance to full ROM at 6+ weeks; avoid horizontal adduction stretching for 6 weeks if distal clavicle resection included; throwers need throwing program before RTS

Surgery Type: SLAP Repair / Bankart Repair / Biceps Tenodesis (2023 Update)
WB Status: NWB surgical UE
Key Restrictions: Sling 6 weeks; sleep in sling 3 weeks; no active ER/extension/abduction; no resisted biceps (elbow flexion/forearm supination) for 6 weeks; no biceps loading until week 10; no throwing/overhead sports until week 20; RTS (contact sports) no sooner than 5 months
Timeline / Phases: Phase I - Protective (0-6 wks): sling, PROM (flex/scaption 90°, ER 30°, IR 45°), no active ER/ext/abd, isometrics; Phase II - Moderate Protection (7-12 wks): isotonic RC strengthening, proprioceptive progressions, biceps AROM/submaximal isometrics at wk 10; Phase III - Minimum Protection (13-20 wks): full ROM, plyometrics, no throwing until wk 20; Phase IV - Advanced (21-26 wks): interval sports, throwing from mound wks 24-28; Isokinetic testing criteria: ER/IR ratio 70%, bilateral ER 98%, bilateral IR 105%
Notes: 2023 update of existing protocol; no biceps loading until week 10; no throwing/overhead sports until week 20; contact sports no sooner than 5 months; isokinetic testing required for throwing athletes with specific passing criteria; 4 phases with clinical milestones to advance

WB Status: NWB surgical UE
Key Restrictions: Sling at all times except HEP and PT; sleep in sling; do not reach arm behind back; IR limited to 45° until 12 weeks post-op; no lifting/pulling/pushing >2 lbs until Phase 3; no overhead work until Phase 4
Timeline / Phases: 0-2 wks: full sling, pendulums, wrist/elbow ROM, gripping; 2-4 wks: sling, PROM supervised (flex 60°, ext 0°, abd 90°, ER 45°, IR 0°), scapular strengthening, isometrics; 4-6 wks: sling removable at night, PROM progressing (flex 90°, ext 30°, abd full, ER 45° at 90°, IR 30° at 90°), AAROM wall walks, Theraband ER; 6-8 wks: sling off, limit IR 45° until 12 wks, full AROM except IR, isotonic RC (6-8 lbs), UBE/wall push-ups, PNF D1/D2 ≤3 lbs; 8-10 wks: isokinetics with 60° block, prone scaption/horizontal abd, body blade; 10-12 wks: advance IR toward full at 12 wks, strengthening 8-10 lbs, Thrower's 10 for athletes; 12-14 wks: full ROM, weight training; 14+ wks (throwers): interval throwing, isokinetic testing at 16-24 wks
Notes: Key restriction: IR limited to 45° until 12 weeks (posterior labral protection); no reaching behind back; sling worn at all times except therapy; weight training begins week 12-14; throwers: interval throwing from week 14-16; contact sports no sooner than 5 months; isokinetic testing criteria same as anterior labral protocol (ER/IR ratio 70%, bilateral ER 98%, IR 105%)

WB Status: NWB surgical UE; abduction pillow brace/sling for 6 weeks
Key Restrictions: Abduction pillow brace/sling for 6 weeks; PT typically not started until 2-week post-op visit; PROM only for first 6 weeks; no isotonics until arm can elevate without shoulder or scapular hiking; progressive strengthening (0.5 kg/10 days, no pain)
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (0-6 wks): Days 0-14: pendulums, elbow/hand gripping, cryotherapy; Wks 2-4: PROM (flex/abd 90°, ER/IR 30°, ext 30°), rhythmic stabilization, scapular isometrics; Wks 4-5: PROM progressing (flex/abd 120°, ER 30°, IR 45°), ER/IR tubing, prone rowing; Wks 5-6: advance PROM, AAROM/AROM to 90°; Phase II (wks 7-14): AROM, lateral raises, full can, prone exercises, UBE (wks 8-9), isotonic resistance 0.5 kg from wk 10, cross-body stretch; Phase III (wks 15-24): full ROM stretches, fundamental shoulder exercises (diagonals, push-up plus, dynamic hug, ER/IR); Phase IV (wks 24-36): return to activity/sport progression
Notes: Abduction pillow brace/sling for full 6 weeks; PT typically delayed until 2-week visit; no isotonics until elevation without scapular hiking; resistance progressed at 0.5 kg/10 days without pain; cross-body posterior capsule stretch in Phase III; return to activity Phase IV (24-36 weeks); 4 phases

WB Status: NWB surgical UE
